docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:181:Auto-recovery is a distinct stage that runs **between** Layer 3 (queue drain) and the `status=proposed` operator-review escape. It is commonly triggered when a bead's within-cycle escalation ladder has been exhausted on two or more consecutive drain cycles (`Extra["consecutive_ladder_exhaustions"] >= 2`; see TD-031 §5 (`consecutive_ladder_exhaustions` Policy) field). The counter is a coordination hint, not a brittle gate: DDx may derive the same condition from attempt/review events, and explicit too-large, needs-decomposition, child-depth-cap, or no-changes-decompose classifications may enter auto-recovery immediately per TD-031 §3.3. The bead must be `status=open` and not carry a valid operator-authored `recovery:manual` label.
docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:190:2. **Decompose attempt** (only when reframe returned no change or the reframer invocation failed) — dispatch a strong-power decomposer agent with the same `MinPower` floor. The decomposer returns 2–5 executable child bead specs when the bead can be split under itself. If child depth is exhausted, it returns sibling or replacement bead specs under the nearest safe parent/root. DDx calls `Store.Create` for each generated bead, wires the required dependency or supersession edges, records `decompose-applied` (TD-027 §13), and leaves the oversized bead `status=open` with `Extra["execution-eligible"]=false` when it should no longer be claimed directly. The queue advances through the generated executable work.
docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:191:3. **Final escape** (only when child decomposition and sibling/replacement decomposition returned no executable work or failed) — DDx records `auto-recovery-failed` (TD-027 §13), moves the bead to `status=proposed`, clears the active claim, and does not schedule another attempt. The operator must resolve the bead via `ddx bead update --status open` (after fixing it) or `ddx bead update --status cancelled`.
docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:195:The auto-recovery sequence runs within the layer-3 run record as an additional stop-condition evaluation entry. The entry names the bead, the trigger condition (`consecutive_ladder_exhaustions` value), which recovery step was attempted, the outcome, and the cost of any reframer/decomposer invocations. These costs contribute to the drain-level budget cap (FEAT-014).
docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:199:- **TD-031 §2** — `reframe_applied`, `decompose_applied`, `auto_recovery_failed`, and `per_bead_budget_exhausted` outcome rows define the status transitions and Extra mutations.
docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:202:- **TD-031 §4 (Auto-Recovery Role Catalogue)** — reframer and decomposer dispatch contracts.

docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:285:For successful replacement rewrites, the bead body may be materially shorter or longer than the original when prompt fitness requires it. Preservation is proven by the `triage-rewritten` / `intake-rewritten` evidence record and durable anchors, not by keeping old text inside the prompt body. For rejected rewrites, readiness enters the §3.3 decomposition-first sequence when structural follow-up work can preserve explicit scope; it moves the bead to `status=proposed` only when safe rewrite/decomposition/replacement cannot resolve the ambiguity without operator judgment.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:9:{"acceptance":"1. `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` returns `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global`, honoring an overridden XDG_DATA_HOME, verified by TestGlobalDir_HonorsXDGDataHome.\n2. `registry.ResolvePlugin` returns layer \"project\" with the project path when both project and global copies exist — TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal asserts the project copy wins.\n3. `registry.ResolvePlugin` returns layer \"global\" with the global path when the project copy is absent but the global copy exists — TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal.\n4. `registry.ResolvePlugin` returns layer \"baked-in\" for the default `ddx` plugin when neither project nor global copy exists, and a non-nil error for any other unresolved name — TestPluginLookup_BakedInDefaultOnly.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/ddxroot/... ./internal/registry/... green.\n6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"f4f79067f34de800edcb3151a2ed74b51407bb2b","created_at":"2026-06-02T01:50:49.073172731Z","description":"PROBLEM\nDDx has no runtime concept of a \"global\" plugin install nor a precedence order for resolving a plugin name. Every install/lookup path is hardcoded to the per-project tree via ddxroot.Path(). Before `--global` install (sibling bead) or `ddx init --global` (sibling bead) can exist, there must be a single resolver that, given a plugin name, returns its on-disk path by checking the project layer first, then a global layer, then the baked-in default.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/registry/installer.go:66 `InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, src, projectRoot)` only knows the project root; there is no global target.\n- cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:75 `Path()` resolves only the project root (in-tree `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx` or convention `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/...` at line 86). There is no `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global` helper.\n- cli/internal/skills/install.go:28 `Install(src, projectRoot, opts)` and all callers assume a single project destination.\n- No `ResolvePlugin`-style function exists anywhere under cli/internal/registry.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Add `GlobalDir()` to cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go returning `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global` (reuse the existing `xdgDataHome()` helper at ddxroot.go:89). The plugins root is `GlobalDir()/plugins`.\n2. Add `registry.ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name string) (path string, layer string, error)` implementing precedence:\n   - `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/\u003cname\u003e` if the dir exists → layer \"project\".\n   - `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e` if it exists → layer \"global\".\n   - baked-in default (defaultplugin.FS, the `ddx` plugin only) → layer \"baked-in\"; return an error for any other unresolved name.\n3. Export the layer string so doctor/init/install (sibling beads) can report which layer answered.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Wiring `--global` into install/update commands (sibling bead: install/update --global).\n- `ddx init --global` and agent-tier symlinks (sibling bead: init topology).\n- `ddx doctor` reporting (sibling bead: doctor + specs).\n- Removing the existing per-project install. Both layers coexist.\n\nDEPS\nddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — `ddxroot.Path()` and the new `GlobalDir()` must share the same XDG_DATA_HOME convention agreed there. Sibling beads in this set depend on this resolver.","events_attachment":"ddx-04566a8f/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-04566a8f","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config","area:plugin","area:cli","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-20de9a81","status":"closed","title":"registry: add two-layer plugin resolution (project → global → baked-in) with global XDG path helper","updated_at":"2026-06-02T01:56:37.269486624Z"}

Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, \".ddx\", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.\n\nDESIGN\n\nddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:\n- \u003cprojectRoot\u003e/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)\n- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/\u003chost\u003e/\u003cowner\u003e/\u003crepo\u003e/ — otherwise (convention mode)\n\nProject identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:\n- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx\n- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx\n- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo\n- Local (no remote) → local/\u003cbasename\u003e-\u003cshort-hash-of-abs-path\u003e\n\nThe XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.\n\nWORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)\n\nEach XDG project root maintains \u003cxdg\u003e/worktrees.json:\n\n  {\"paths\": [{\"path\": \"/Users/erik/Projects/ddx\", \"first_seen_at\": \"...\", \"last_seen_at\": \"...\", \"hostname\": \"...\"}], \"master\": \"/Users/erik/Projects/ddx\"}\n\n- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.\n- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.\n- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.\n- `ddx project worktree master \u003cpath\u003e` re-pins.\n- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.\n\nDDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.\n\nAUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal \".ddx\" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:\n- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)\n- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)\n- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)\n- cli/internal/server/ (workers, federation, GraphQL adapters)\n- cli/internal/config/ (resolved, types)\n- cli/internal/triage/, cli/internal/metaprompt/, cli/internal/registry/\n- library/checks/ (where ddx-side paths exist)\n\nA regression linter (lefthook hook or go vet analyzer) blocks any PR that introduces a new filepath.Join(..., \".ddx\", ...) literal outside cli/internal/ddxroot/.\n\nSPEC IMPACT — the following docs reference .ddx/ paths and must be updated in this bead:\n- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md (state paths)\n- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-019-multi-project-server-topology.md (project identity)\n- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-020-multi-machine-coordinator-topology.md (project identity)\n- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-021-operator-prompt-beads-web-write-path.md (operator-prompt paths)\n- docs/helix/02-design/workers-sessions-operator-surface.md (worker status paths)\n- docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md (FEAT-010 runs/)\n\nEach spec update: replace .ddx/\u003cpath\u003e citations with ddxroot.Path()/\u003cpath\u003e AND add a \"project identity\" subsection where applicable referencing this bead.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Skills/personas install strategy (principle 2 — separate bead).\n- Test matrix (separate bead).\n- Migrating today's in-tree ddx-repo to convention mode (out of scope; this repo stays in-tree as the dogfooding canon).\n\nDEPS\nNone. This is the foundational refactor.","events_attachment":"ddx-06cbaa90/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-06cbaa90","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config","area:refactor","kind:refactor","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:ddx-home"],"needs-human-reason":"operator decision needed. Non-Linux behavior is covered by `TestExecutorParentDeathSignalNonLinuxFallbackDocumented` or build-tagged documentation explaining the fallback and why `Pdeathsig` is unavailable.\n6. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/...` passes.\n7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"133c2ebe9d9d247d4384dfb3655d20abbae0b0c2","created_at":"2026-05-27T09:49:01.740020404Z","description":"PROBLEM\nWhen a `ddx work` orchestrator dies abnormally (SIGKILL, crash, OOM, or supervising-process loss) while an execute-bead attempt is running, the spawned harness child can reparent to PID 1 and continue indefinitely. Observed 2026-05-27: a `claude --print` execute-bead child remained orphaned with PPID 1 for ~9h53m after its `ddx work` parent exited.\n\nROOT CAUSE (file:line)\n- cli/internal/agent/executor.go:156 spawns the harness via `exec.Command(binary, args...)` and then calls `cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd)`.\n- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:13-15 sets only `SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}`. There is no Linux `Pdeathsig`, so the kernel does not signal the harness when the parent dies abnormally.\n- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:20-25 kills the process group only from graceful executor paths.\n- cli/cmd/root.go:49-54 uses `signal.NotifyContext` for os.Interrupt and SIGTERM only. SIGKILL and crashes cannot be caught, so executor cleanup does not run.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nOn Linux, set `SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig` to SIGKILL or SIGTERM for execute-bead harness commands in `cmdSetProcessGroup`. Handle the Go runtime thread-migration caveat with `runtime.LockOSThread` around process start or an equivalent parent-PID guard so the parent-death signal is attached to the intended parent thread. Preserve process-group setup so existing graceful cancellation and timeout cleanup still kill the whole harness tree. Document the macOS/Windows fallback because `Pdeathsig` is Linux-only.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown already works through executor.go:231-255; do not regress it.\n- The harness-runner-internal leak in fizeau is covered by fizeau-50b6f055; this child bead is only the DDx orchestrator-to-harness-child boundary.\n- Startup orphan reaping is covered by a separate child bead from this decomposition.\n- docker-clone backend cleanup is covered by a separate child bead from this decomposition.\n\nCROSS-REF\n- fizeau-50b6f055: fizeau harness-runner process-group cleanup.\n- ddx-8f2e0ebf: lease release / operator-attention.","events_attachment":"ddx-0d7d5f0e/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-0d7d5f0e","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:workers","kind:bug","kind:reliability","priority:reliability","severity:high","observability"],"parent":"ddx-93d2ac52","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-f490694e","status":"closed","title":"fix executor: enforce parent-death cleanup for execute-bead harness children on Linux","updated_at":"2026-05-27T10:14:33.399641365Z"}

This produces an unbreakable deadlock:\n\n  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --\u003e child_A\n  e29892a4 -- parent --\u003e child_B\n  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           \u003c- BAD: parent is also a dep\n  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  \u003c- BAD\n\nThe parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.\n\nObserved scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):\n- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')\n- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)\n- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]\n- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores\n- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order\n\nRoot cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. TestConventionMode_ReadsGlobalSkillsBeforeHardcoded asserts that with --global installed, convention mode picks up the global plugin instead of the hardcoded one.\n6. ddx doctor's \"Global Install\" check exists and reports the install path + status.\n7. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/config/... green.\n8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-15T01:09:39.694724565Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-15c71970","depends_on_id":"ddx-564ea1cf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-15T01:10:33Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-15c71970","depends_on_id":"ddx-ad338ec3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-15T01:10:34Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM\nConvention mode (per ddx-ad338ec3) bootstraps an XDG project dir but has no source for skills, personas, or the default ddx plugin. Either every project re-downloads the plugin on first use, or the user must have a global install. The latter is the better answer: install once per machine, all convention-mode projects share.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nThere is no per-user install path today. `ddx init` only writes per-project state. Skills/personas live in the project's .ddx/plugins/ (in-tree) or are missing entirely (convention).\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n\n1. Add `ddx init --global` flag (and corresponding code path in cli/cmd/init.go). When set:\n   - Resolve target path: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ (defaults to ~/.local/share/ddx/global/).\n   - Install the default ddx plugin (library/ contents) into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/.\n   - Write a default ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults (library URL, branch, etc.).\n   - Idempotent: re-running upgrades to a newer plugin version but does not destroy operator state.\n\n2. Convention mode reads ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ as the second precedence tier (in-tree \u003e global \u003e hardcoded). If --global has never been run, fall back to baked-in hardcoded defaults — convention mode still works without it; --global is an optimization for users who want offline-cacheable plugin content.\n\n3. Document the install path in ddx doctor: a new check \"Global Install\" reports whether ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ exists and points operators to `ddx init --global` if it does not.\n\n4. Do NOT remove the existing per-project plugin install (in-tree mode at .ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/). Both coexist.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- In-project ddx init manifest write (E4 bead).\n- Migration from legacy ~/.config/ddx/ or ~/.ddx/ if such exist on disk (none expected; today there is no global install).\n- Plugin update / upgrade flow for the global install (use existing `ddx update` mechanism; verify it works against the new path).\n\nDEPS\nddx-564ea1cf (E1) — resolver must exist.\nddx-ad338ec3 (E2) — convention mode reads from this path.","id":"ddx-15c71970","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config","area:cli","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint"],"notes":"Superseded by epic restructure into 3 consolidated beads aligned to the two principles: (1) bulletproof ddx home resolution, (2) centralized vs in-project skills/personas. New beads pending.","parent":"ddx-602ae31a","priority":2,"schema_version":1,"status":"cancelled","title":"config: ddx init --global installs user-tier skills/personas to XDG global","updated_at":"2026-05-15T01:16:05.901133882Z"}

Every claim attempt produces the same warn-only rejection, so the worker logs a warning and skips. With 23 ready beads in the fizeau queue and one fizeau-scoped daemon, the entire queue is idle indefinitely.\n\nOBSERVATION (Fizeau, 2026-05-18 05:27-05:39 UTC; about an hour after the previous wedge bug ddx-8f2e0ebf)\n- Worker: PID 1527216, ddx work --watch in /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau, etime 01:22:40 at observation.\n- Log: /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/agent-logs/agent-loop-1779077845655338391.jsonl\n- Pattern every ~50s: resource.preflight (status ok) -\u003e pre_claim_intake.start -\u003e pre_claim_intake.warn -\u003e loop.idle.\n- Sample pre_claim_intake.warn payload:\n  {\n    \"bead_id\":\"fizeau-5b2a8950\",\n    \"decision\":\"warn\",\n    \"decision_source\":\"pre_claim_intake\",\n    \"detail\":\"decode result: json: cannot unmarshal bool into Go struct field .readiness_checks.verdict of type string\",\n    \"fingerprint\":\"15155a1cd44e96daf53eabb14c8eb3ccd8b0e5271d881208cfcbbf2869899f4f\",\n    \"outcome\":\"intake_error\",\n    \"policy_mode\":\"warn-only\",\n    \"reason\":\"system_unready\",\n    \"rule_id\":\"pre_claim_intake.system_unready\",\n    \"suggested_action\":\"check the readiness route or harness configuration and retry\",\n    \"system_reason\":\"unavailable\"\n  }\n- Identical fingerprint across multiple bead_ids confirms it is a schema mismatch, not a bead-specific issue.\n- Queue state at observation: open=44, ready=23, dependency-waiting=19. None of the 23 ready beads got claimed in the prior 30 minutes; commit log empty for the same window.\n- Other beads (fizeau-a15106ab, fizeau-90b85c8b) WERE successfully closed earlier in the night through some other path (perhaps a different worker version or a different execute-bead invocation). Suggests this decoder bug appeared mid-session OR only affects certain readiness routes.\n\nWHY THIS IS A BUG\n1. Schema drift between the readiness-check producer and the pre_claim_intake consumer. One emits verdict as bool, the other parses it as string. .ddx/beads.jsonl:87:{"acceptance":"1. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestExecuteBead.*Cleanup|TestCleanupAttemptWorktree' -count=1` passes.\n2. A unit test exists for each of these non-success outcomes asserting WorktreeRemove was called: provider_connectivity, no_evidence_produced, execution_failed, structural_validation_failed, land_conflict, post_run_check_failed.\n3. A unit test asserts the success-path classifier does NOT call WorktreeRemove from the new hook (landing path retains ownership).\n4. A unit test asserts `ddx try --no-merge` preserves the worktree regardless of outcome.\n5. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./... -count=1` passes.\n6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"e979bcd786ff98bd4767596dd1ed2dbe76cddb0c","created_at":"2026-05-29T02:02:14.061932044Z","description":"PROBLEM: When a `ddx try`/`ddx work` attempt fails with any non-success\noutcome (`provider_connectivity`, `no_evidence_produced`, `execution_failed`,\n`structural_validation_failed`, `land_conflict`, `post_run_check_failed`),\nits execute-bead worktree under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-\u003cbead\u003e-\u003cattemptID\u003e/`\nis not removed. Cleanup happens only when a *subsequent attempt of the same bead*\nis launched and `RecoverOrphans` runs as pre-spawn sweep.\n\nOBSERVED 2026-05-28 in niflheim drain: a 6.6 GB worktree\n`.execute-bead-wt-niflheim-07f6f8ff-20260528T164209-f179349f` remained 9+\nhours after its attempt failed with `provider_connectivity` (bragi 502).\nThe bead's retry resolved as `already_satisfied` without spawning a new\nexecute-bead worktree (the AC was already met by partially-landed commits\nfrom the failed attempt), so `RecoverOrphans` was never called for that\nbead — the leak is permanent until another attempt at the same bead runs.\n\nThe user reports seeing this pattern consistently across drains, not just\non the one observed bead.\n\nROOT CAUSE: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:393-411`\ndefines `RecoverOrphans` with the docstring \"the parent's responsibility —\ncall it before spawning new workers so stale worktrees from crashed previous\nattempts do not accumulate.\" The function matches strictly by\n`basenamePrefix := ExecuteBeadWtPrefix + beadID + \"-\"` and is only invoked\non a new-attempt pre-spawn path. Two leak conditions follow:\n\n  (a) Bead's retry succeeds via `already_satisfied` (no new worktree\n      spawned because the AC check resolves before agent invocation).\n      The orphan from the prior failed attempt is never matched.\n  (b) Bead is closed for any reason after a failed attempt and is never\n      re-attempted (most common in real flows — failures often resolve\n      via decomposition into different child beads, never returning to\n      the original).\n\nThe post-attempt outcome classifier at\n`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1966` (Classify worker outcome:\ntask_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes / ...) is the natural place\nto call `WorktreeRemove` for non-success classes, but does not.\nThe existing landing path at\n`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1088,1329,1679` calls\n`gitOps.RemoveWorktree` correctly for landing-time temp worktrees, but\nthose are different (per-landing) worktrees, not the per-attempt execute-bead\nworktree under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/`.\n\nPROPOSED FIX: Add an attempt-finalize cleanup hook that runs after outcome\nclassification (around `execute_bead.go:1966-2017`) and removes the\nper-attempt execute-bead worktree when the outcome is anything other than\n`task_succeeded`. Preserve only when:\n  1. The operator explicitly opted in via `ddx try --no-merge` (preserve\n     for inspection regardless of outcome).\n  2. An `executions_mirror.go` write or evidence-capture step requires the\n     worktree to persist past classification (verify by reading the call\n     chain at `execute_bead.go:2069-2114`).\n\nImplementation steps the executing agent should follow:\n  1. Read `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1423-2017` to confirm the\n     outcome-classification flow and identify the right post-classify hook\n     point.\n  2. Add a helper `cleanupAttemptWorktree(gitOps, workDir, wtPath, outcome,\n     preserveFlag)` that calls `gitOps.WorktreeRemove(workDir, wtPath)` for\n     non-success outcomes unless `preserveFlag` is set.\n  3. Wire it into the outcome-classification path.\n  4. Add a unit test asserting the cleanup branch for each non-success\n     outcome class, and a negative test confirming success-path keeps the\n     worktree (landing path owns that cleanup).\n  5. Update `RecoverOrphans` docstring to reflect that it's now a\n     belt-and-suspenders sweep, not the primary cleanup path.\n\nNON-SCOPE:\n- Changing `RecoverOrphans` behavior or call sites (keep it; it covers\n  hard-crash cases the new hook can't reach).\n- Adding a periodic background sweeper or `ddx doctor` flag for stale\n  worktrees (potential follow-up).\n- Changing the worktree path under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/`.\n- Changing outcome-classification logic or outcome taxonomy.\n- Adding telemetry/metrics for leak rates.\n\nIN-SCOPE FILES:\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (add the cleanup hook + helper)\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_test.go or sibling _test file (add\n  cleanup tests for each non-success outcome)\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go (docstring update on\n  RecoverOrphans only; behavior unchanged)\n\nOUT-OF-SCOPE FILES:\n- execute_bead_land.go — landing-time temp-worktree cleanup is correct\n  and untouched.\n- Any code under cli/cmd/ — no CLI surface change required.\n- docs/ — documentation update is downstream of the spec-evolution bead\n  ddx-828a1b8f (worktree resource isolation) and not required here.","events_attachment":"ddx-274943fc/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-274943fc","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:execution","kind:bug","kind:operability","phase:iterate","spec:FEAT-010"],"needs-human-reason":"consecutive_wedge","needs-human-since":"2026-05-31T05:20:40Z","needs-human-source":"ddx work","needs-human-suggested-action":"investigate the repeated wedge (resolver hang / phase-empty heartbeats) and clear the operator-attention state once resolved","needs-human-summary":"bead wedged on 2 consecutive claims (\u003e= threshold 2; last wedge \"progress_watchdog\"); stopped re-claiming and flagged for operator attention","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-90ab9f5e","spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: clean up worktree on non-success attempt outcomes (currently leaks on failed-then-retried-as-already_satisfied path)","updated_at":"2026-06-06T02:28:44.767198655Z"}

JSON tags drive both REST wire format and disk persistence.\n- Mode is the canonical once/drain/watch selector. CLI --once maps to Mode=once. CLI --watch maps to Mode=watch. agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=8503 cost_usd=0.8610"},{"actor":"erik","body":"Architectural conflict with open bead ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7), plus the bead materially exceeds the size-check thresholds. Human triage required to resolve the design conflict before any unification work commits. ## Summary The bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector. These are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either: (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned. The bead's \"SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS\" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make. ## Evidence 1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims). 2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: \"Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands.\" Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`. 3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites). 4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (\"ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server\") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. The remaining server-spawn path is via `POST /api/agent/workers/work` (`cli/internal/server/server.go:2390`) and the GraphQL `workerDispatchAdapter` (`cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127`), both of which ADR-022 step 7 wants to delete in favor of subprocess exec. 5. Recent ddx-eccc6efb attempt (20260503T065047-61e4ed20, result.json) was DECLINED for size and decomposed into proposed children 7a/7b/7c — those children may not yet be filed in the main repo per its own bookkeeping note, but the intent stands. ## Size-check verdict (independent of the conflict) The bead also fails the size check on its own: - 17 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (cobra flag binding, client-side validation, HTTP handler, GraphQL adapter, workers.go consumer, on-disk persistence, backward-compat for legacy fields, forward-compat with SpecVersion, custom Duration codec, reflection-driven test, manual end-to-end run). - Touches at minimum: new package `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/` (struct + Validate + ApplyDefaults + custom Duration + tests), `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (cobra rebind + dispatch split), `cli/internal/server/server.go` (handler rewrite + handler tests), `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` + `graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go` + `workers_config_test.go` (parallel-construction deletion), `cli/internal/server/workers.go` (consumer rewrite), and a literal sweep across at least 8 test files (`workers_test.go`, `workers_e2e_test.go`, `workers_prune_test.go`, `workers_stop_test.go`, `workers_stop_propagation_test.go`, `workers_watchdog_test.go`, plus the two GraphQL test files) that all construct `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{}` literals. - File-size signal: workers.go 1959 LOC, server.go 5248 LOC, agent_cmd.go 2245 LOC, workers_test.go 1107 LOC. Estimated change footprint comfortably \u003e500 LOC across \u003e5 files in unrelated packages. - Per execution-bead protocol size check: \"More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems\" + \"More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages\" → DECOMPOSE. ## Why I am not decomposing in this attempt The protocol's decomposition path commits the project to a particular shape (in this case: unification rather than deletion). Filing 4–6 child beads under ddx-29058e2a would advance an architectural direction that is in unresolved conflict with ddx-eccc6efb. A clean decomposition only makes sense AFTER the parent-vs-step-7 design choice is made by a human: Option A — Drop ddx-29058e2a in favor of ADR-022 step 7. The parallel-struct smell goes away by deletion, not unification. The 5 currently-dropped flags can be patched as a narrow fix on the existing struct in the meantime if the operator workflow needs them, with a note that step 7 will erase the struct. Option B — Keep ddx-29058e2a and amend / close ddx-eccc6efb. The unified spec becomes the canonical wire format; ADR-022 step 7 reduces in scope (or is closed as superseded) because the server keeps marshalling a spec, just a unified one. Option C — Sequence: land the narrow 5-flag fix now (small bead), then land ADR-022 step 7 (which deletes the struct and obsoletes the parallel-struct smell), then close ddx-29058e2a as superseded. Each option has different downstream implications (ddx-fb290074 audit scope, REACH-PROTO entry roots, where Validate/ApplyDefaults live, whether SpecVersion is needed at all). I do not have authority to pick. ## Recommended next actions for the orchestrator / triage 1. Decide which of A / B / C above is the intended path. This is a 5-minute design call, not a research task. 2. If A: close ddx-29058e2a as superseded; optionally file a small fix bead \"add MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec + propagate through handler\" (~50 LOC, will be deleted by step 7 anyway). 3. If B: amend ddx-eccc6efb's scope (or close it), then decompose ddx-29058e2a into ~5 children: (i) new spec package + Validate/ApplyDefaults + Duration codec + reflection test, (ii) cobra rebind + parseExecuteLoopSpec + dispatch split, (iii) HTTP handler decode-into-spec rewrite + delete handler-local request struct, (iv) GraphQL adapter unification, (v) workers.go consumer migration + workers_*_test literal sweep + spec.json on-disk format flip + SpecVersion gate. 4. If C: file the narrow 5-flag fix as its own bead now; pause ddx-29058e2a until after step 7 lands, at which point most of its ACs disappear and what's left (if anything) can be re-scoped. ## What this attempt did NOT do (and why) - No code changes. The conflict is upstream of any implementation choice. - No child beads filed. Filing children would commit to the unification path; see \"Why I am not decomposing\" above. - No partial fix to the 5 dropped flags. .ddx/beads.jsonl:97:{"acceptance":"1. `TestInstall_InTreeWritesProjectTreeAndLinks` (cli/cmd/install_test.go:21) asserts `ddx install \u003cname\u003e` in in-tree mode writes to `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/` and creates project-tier links under `\u003cproject\u003e/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e` and `\u003cproject\u003e/.agents/skills/\u003cname\u003e`.\n2. `TestInstall_ConventionModeWritesXDGProjectTree` (cli/cmd/install_test.go:73) asserts `ddx install \u003cname\u003e` in convention mode writes to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/\u003chost\u003e/\u003cowner\u003e/\u003crepo\u003e/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/` and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins path.\n3. `TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode` (cli/cmd/update_test.go:247) asserts `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.\n4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... green.\n5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"8fbb32ad36b842cefed25f048cd5511a8b64afe9","created_at":"2026-06-03T11:21:13.13129759Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a, slice 3 of 4)\n\nWithout `--global`, install and update must target the project's ddx root, which differs by mode: in-tree (`\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e`) versus convention (`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/\u003chost\u003e/\u003cowner\u003e/\u003crepo\u003e/plugins/\u003cname\u003e`). Either way the project-tier agent skill links (`\u003cproject\u003e/.claude/skills` and `\u003cproject\u003e/.agents/skills`) must point into whichever plugins tree is active.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- `resolveInstallDirs(global=false)` (cli/cmd/install.go:184, project branch install.go:195-197) sets `installRoot = ddxroot.Path(ctx, f.WorkingDir)` and skill dirs to `\u003cworkingDir\u003e/.agents/skills` and `\u003cworkingDir\u003e/.claude/skills`. `ddxroot.Path` (cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:75) returns the in-tree `.ddx` path when `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx` exists and the convention `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/...` path otherwise, so mode selection is implicit.\n- `installLocal` non-global branch derives `ddxRootDir := ddxroot.Path(...)` (install.go:453) for the same routing.\n- Update: the non-global path `performUpdate` (cli/cmd/update.go:204) uses `ddxroot.JoinProject(workingDir, ...)` (e.g. update.go:227) so it updates the project install in whichever mode is active; mode is not an explicit flag but follows `ddxroot`.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nEnsure `ddx install \u003cname\u003e` (no `--global`) in in-tree mode writes to `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/` and links `\u003cproject\u003e/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e` and `\u003cproject\u003e/.agents/skills/\u003cname\u003e`; in convention mode (no `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/`) writes to the XDG project plugins path and links the project-tier skill dirs into that path. Ensure `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- The `--global` install/update surface (slice 2).\n- `ddx init` behavior and doctor reporting (slice 4).\n- Removing either mode — in-tree and convention coexist.\n\nDEPS\n- Slice 1 (plugin lookup precedence) and ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — project paths route through `ddxroot.Path()`.","events_attachment":"ddx-2b9c26d7/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-2b9c26d7","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:plugin","area:cli","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas","parent:ddx-602ae31a","dep:ddx-06cbaa90"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-c7bae1d6","status":"closed","title":"project install surface: ddx install (no flag) and ddx update honor in-tree vs convention mode","updated_at":"2026-06-03T12:16:40.973821743Z","work-no-changes-count":1}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:103:{"acceptance":"1. TestReadinessChecksSchema in cli/internal/agent/readiness_schema_test.go validates every verdict form the decoder accepts (bool true, bool false, string, null, absent) against cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json AND asserts an object verdict is rejected, locking producer/consumer alignment.\n2. The producer prompt buildPreClaimIntakePrompt (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:636-655) references the canonical schema path via the readinessChecksSchemaPath constant (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:21), not a duplicated string literal.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... green\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes","closing_commit_sha":"rev-ddx-2e3ad63d","created_at":"2026-05-28T09:08:21.2215798Z","description":"PROBLEM\nThe pre_claim_intake readiness payload schema can drift between the producer prompt and the Go decoder. That drift is exactly the class of bug that wedged the fizeau queue: the producer emitted readiness_checks[].verdict as a JSON bool while the consumer parsed a string, so every claim attempt failed with a warn-only rejection and the queue idled. There must be a single shared schema that both sides consume, plus a CI-run test that fails the moment the schema and decoder diverge.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- The producer prompt references the schema only by a path string in prose: cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:639 (buildPreClaimIntakePrompt) writes \"Canonical schema: \"+readinessChecksSchemaPath, and the constant lives at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:21. Because the prompt text and the schema file are independent, instructions can drift from the schema with nothing catching it.\n- The consumer decoder accepts bool/string/null/absent verdict forms: readinessVerdict.UnmarshalJSON at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:97-125 and preClaimReadinessChecksPayload.UnmarshalJSON at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:386-416.\n- The schema verdict union is cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json:44-50 (oneOf boolean/string/null). Nothing fails CI if someone tightens the schema, or changes the decoder, so that the two diverge.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nKeep cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json as the single source of truth consumed by both the producer prompt (via the readinessChecksSchemaPath constant) and the consumer decoder. Ensure a CI-run test validates every verdict form the decoder accepts against the schema and asserts that an object verdict is rejected, so any tightening of the schema or change to the decoder that breaks alignment fails the suite. Confirm the producer prompt references the canonical path constant rather than a duplicated literal.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Do NOT implement the runtime same-fingerprint warn escalation guard (owned by the sibling child bead).\n- Do NOT change the verdict coercion semantics (bool true-\u003epass, bool false-\u003efail, string-\u003elowercased, null/absent-\u003eempty).\n- Do NOT introduce go.mod replace directives.\n\nDEPENDENCIES: no deps.","events_attachment":"ddx-2e3ad63d/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-2e3ad63d","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:pre-claim","kind:reliability","observability","priority:reliability","decomposed","needs-human-decomposition","triage:no-changes-unjustified"],"parent":"ddx-fd02cf6e","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"Lock readiness_checks schema as the shared producer/consumer source of truth with a CI drift gate","updated_at":"2026-05-28T10:31:56.276408092Z","work-no-changes-count":1}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:105:{"acceptance":"1. `TestLefthookPreCommitIncludesDDxRootAudit` passes and asserts `lefthook.yml` wires the DDx-root audit into `pre-commit`.\n2. `rg 'filepath\\.Join\\([^)]*\"\\.ddx\"' cli/ --type go | grep -v '^cli/internal/ddxroot/'` returns zero hits.\n3. `lefthook run pre-commit` fails when a staged Go file reintroduces a hardcoded `filepath.Join(..., \".ddx\", ...)` outside `cli/internal/ddxroot/`.\n4. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/server/...` passes.\n5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"7786b6345ebe1d238bf6f5e4c4a4225d614b1bbf","created_at":"2026-05-15T06:00:35.425545865Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-2f93f5d9","depends_on_id":"ddx-e4c3d791","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-15T06:00:57Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-2f93f5d9","depends_on_id":"ddx-b6fea19a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-15T06:00:58Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-2f93f5d9","depends_on_id":"ddx-3bbf066b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-15T06:00:58Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-2f93f5d9","depends_on_id":"ddx-16fcc557","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-15T06:00:58Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM\nEven after production packages are migrated, the acceptance grep will keep failing because test files and non-test helpers still use literal `.ddx` joins, and `lefthook.yml` does not yet enforce the audit in pre-commit.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nRepresentative test/helper hits remain in `cli/cmd/run_test_helpers.go:206`, `cli/cmd/bead_workspace_test.go:18`, `cli/cmd/work_resource_exhausted_test.go:95`, `cli/internal/server/workers_test.go:311`, and many other `_test.go` fixtures. Strong-model escalation exists, but it is not reliably tied to the class of failure. The worker spent repeated attempts on structural or system failures that stronger models cannot fix, while automatic review evidence was empty on attempts that were recorded as successful. This violates the DDx engineering principle \"Cheapest Viable, Escalate on Evidence\" (docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45): review, runtime, or retry evidence should determine escalation, and only lower-power inability to meet acceptance criteria should push to stronger routes. It also threatens FEAT-010 task-execution reliability because beads can be retried, escalated, or closed without a typed, auditable reason.\n\nThis bead is a Niflheim incident/regression closure bead. It is not a duplicate of the narrower queue items listed under INTERSECTIONS; instead it captures the external evidence DDx workers will not otherwise have, and requires a DDx-side regression suite proving those observed scenarios classify correctly before rerun, escalation, operator attention, or close.\n\nBACKGROUND AND OBSERVED NIFLHEIM EVIDENCE\nThe evidence was gathered from Niflheim worker logs and result bundles, but those files are not load-bearing for this bead. The relevant facts are copied here because an isolated DDx worker cannot access the Niflheim repository or `/tmp` logs.\n\n1. Decomposed parent kept consuming attempts and escalated to a stronger model anyway.\n- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-64217cc5`, title \"Instrument cold replicator with structured per-operation S3 tracing\".\n- Observed result bundle: `.ddx/executions/20260606T152006-a4ca7254/result.json` plus worker logs from 2026-06-05/2026-06-06.\n- Routing showed escalation exists: repeated routes started at `gpt-5.4-mini` with requested min powers 5, 7, 8, and 9, then reached `gpt-5.5` at requested min power 10.\n- Metrics on the bead showed `attempt_count=24`, `total_tokens=19371353`, and average duration about `244112ms`.\n- Despite triage recommending `close_decomposed_or_mark_execution_ineligible`, retries continued until the latest event recorded:\n  `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`\n  `powerClass=standard`\n  `rationale: status: open`\n  `orchestrator_action: decompose`\n  `reason: parent bead exceeds the Step 0 size threshold and is already decomposed into focused child beads for tracing, counters, tests, and runbook work.`\n  `child_beads: niflheim-f03c61a2, niflheim-df407aa8, niflheim-7a421ede, niflheim-0bbed6c9`\n  `outcome_reason=decomposed`\n- Expected DDx behavior: once the worker has reliable evidence that the bead is a decomposed parent or execution-ineligible parent, no further implementation attempt or model escalation should occur. It should mark execution-ineligible/proposed/operator-attention according to policy and move on.\n\n2. Land/evidence coordination failures were treated like implementation failure and retried expensively.\n- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-c75aba30`, title \"Materializer per-merge memory footprint OOMs even at concurrent_limit=8\".\n- Two expensive attempts both used `gpt-5.4-mini`; no useful stronger-model escalation was observed.\n- Metrics showed `attempt_count=2`, `total_tokens=65513510`, and average duration about `3306461.5ms`.\n- First attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T113550-7b29ee42/result.json`.\n  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.\n  - Implementation result commit was `7aaeb0712...`; a merge commit `2c6f4d6e6` was later observed as an ancestor of HEAD, but the result still recorded a land coordination failure.\n  - Error detail:\n    `land coordination failed: land advancer: restoring refs/heads/main to ba2d3059f14e834dcb147f2a6db3b294d83b0fac after evidence commit failed: git update-ref refs/heads/main: fatal: update_ref failed for ref 'refs/heads/main': cannot lock ref 'refs/heads/main': is at 5a176b332b182f2a959614f63769d835c47ffd97 but expected 2c6f4d6e6961b8a93742cde66bceec056934655a`\n  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision=\"success\"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.\n- Second attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T142223-94f8f0f8/result.json`.\n  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.\n  - Error detail:\n    `land coordination failed: land advancer: landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s: A .ddx/executions/20260606T150832-af40450b/result.json`\n  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision=\"success\"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.\n- At observation time the worker had claimed it again (`20260606T152024-a21d8c54`) and edited six files in a fresh isolated worktree:\n  `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/mod.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/tests.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/types.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/config.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/executor.rs`, and `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/tests.rs`.\n- Expected DDx behavior: land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures are system or coordination failures, not model-quality failures. The retry policy should reconcile a successfully landed candidate when possible, retry only the land/evidence step when safe, or park/operator-attention the bead when the landing worktree is dirty. It should not blindly rerun implementation at the same or stronger route.\n\n3. Pre-claim intake/system dirt caused repeated warning churn instead of a clear operator lane.\n- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-a62d31ce`, title \"Publish competitive benchmark artifacts and provenance metadata\".\n- A live worker repeatedly ran readiness/intake. The log showed readiness timeout after five minutes, then:\n  `preserved pre-dispatch implementation changes in /home/erik/Projects/niflheim under refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618; released niflheim-a62d31ce and continuing watch. dirty paths: .ddx/harness-sessions/-2929681.json. recover with git stash apply refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618`\n- Bead events included `pre_claim_intake.warn` with timeout/system-unready reason and generic suggested action.\n- Expected DDx behavior: repeated same/systemic pre-claim warnings and dirty generated DDx artifacts should become an operator-visible/system-unready state after the configured threshold, not an indefinite poll/release loop.\n\n4. Automatic reviews were not forcing meaningful quality improvement.\n- Recent Niflheim \"success\" attempts had empty review results:\n  - `niflheim-9dea95da`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T043655-845c77ee/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision=\"success\"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `78e51a168...`.\n  - `niflheim-da4e1311`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T085358-d80ada3e/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision=\"success\"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `d43be3a3...`.\n  - `niflheim-c75aba30` attempts listed above: `cycle_trace.final_decision=\"success\"` with `review_result={}` despite overall land failures.\n- Manual Claude review earlier improved bead wording and completeness, but the automatic worker path did not show enforceable per-AC review evidence.\n- Expected DDx behavior: unless review is explicitly skipped with a durable reason (`runtime.NoReview` or a valid `review:skip` + reason policy), an empty review result must be a review error or operator-attention signal. It must not be treated as approval, success, or evidence of quality improvement.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\n- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative routing table: only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate; dirty worktree, transport, quota, auth, setup, timeout, and merge-conflict do not escalate. The live Niflheim behavior shows execute-bead/work outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors. The caller can record an overall `execution_failed` without distinguishing landed-candidate reconciliation, ref/index contention, staged evidence dirt, or dirty landing worktree from implementation failure.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off. This permits repeated same-fingerprint/system warnings to look like a live worker instead of a system-unready/operator lane.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2175-2315` handles `PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed`, but missing/empty decomposition paths can warn and continue. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3826-3831` can recognize execution-ineligible/decomposed state only after the bead has the right metadata. The Niflheim parent evidence shows structural/decomposed-parent outcomes can still reach repeated implementation attempts and power escalation.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3042-3138` treats success disposition/status as closeable once candidate-cycle review/landing has allowed success, and the old post-merge review is optional. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:799-823` explicitly proves a worker with `Reviewer: nil` can skip review and close the bead. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:168-193` defines `ReviewResult` with all fields optional except the zero-value enum field; no invariant rejects `{}` as a success-path review artifact.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Add compact Niflheim-style regression fixtures under `cli/internal/agent/testdata/niflheim_worker_evidence/` or equivalent. The fixtures should encode the four scenario families above: empty review success, land coordination/ref-lock/staged-evidence failure, decomposed-parent no-changes rationale with child beads, and repeated pre-claim system-unready warning. Do not depend on Niflheim checkout paths.\n- Introduce or tighten a single execute-bead retry classification boundary that maps attempt result, land result, review result, no-changes rationale, and pre-claim intake outcome into exactly one action: close, retry same tier, escalate min power/profile, reconcile land/evidence, execution-ineligible/proposed, or operator-attention. Wire this boundary into `ddx work`/`ddx try` so `failclass.Classify` is not merely a standalone helper.\n- Enforce review evidence on success. For non-skipped success paths, `cycle_trace.review_result` or the equivalent result field must contain a non-empty verdict, per-AC evidence or documented reason, reviewer routing/session metadata when available, and a classification. `{}` is invalid and must prevent close or produce a review-error/operator-attention event. Explicit review skips remain allowed only when they are durable, reasoned, and visible in result JSON.\n- Classify land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures separately from implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already landed or is an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree has staged generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to the existing local reliability policy; do not escalate the model for this class.\n- Classify decomposed-parent/no-changes rationale as execution-ineligible or operator work before another implementation attempt. Stronger models cannot fix parent beads that are already split into executable children.\n- Make worker result JSON and event bodies carry enough audit fields to explain the decision: `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual power/profile/model, review status/verdict, and land/reconcile status.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names. DDx should continue to pass power/profile intent and let Fizeau choose concrete routes.\n- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.\n- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.\n- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this bead requires typed classification and review evidence, not another timeout implementation.\n- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead uses the Niflheim observation as cross-scenario regression coverage.\n- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond the state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.\n\nINTERSECTIONS\n- Parent: `ddx-3106674f` (multi-worker hardening and local reliability gates). This bead is the external Niflheim regression/incident closure layer under that reliability epic.\n- Dependency: `ddx-2965925e` covers tracker commit index contention. This bead must not reimplement that fix; it must prove land/ref/index/evidence failures classify separately from model failure once the low-level path is hardened.\n- Dependency: `ddx-b2846610` covers bounded post-commit review/land. This bead must still require typed review/land outcomes after the timeout wedge is fixed.\n- Dependency: `ddx-d67f124e` covers same-fingerprint pre-claim warning escalation. This bead adds the Niflheim `niflheim-a62d31ce` family as regression evidence.\n- Related but not a dependency: `ddx-e1a576a7` is an execution-ineligible decomposed parent and a local precedent for the `niflheim-64217cc5` class.\n- Related quality framework: `ddx-a9d130d0` and `ddx-ac10e7af` own broader bead lifecycle/operator-lane policy; this bead is concrete worker behavior and evidence gating.","id":"ddx-334c0958","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:iterate","area:agent","area:execute-bead","area:workers","area:review","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","spec:FEAT-010","spec:TP-021","adr:024","incident:niflheim-2026-06-06","prevention"],"parent":"ddx-3106674f","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"open","title":"execute-bead worker: gate success on review evidence and classify Niflheim retry failures","updated_at":"2026-06-06T15:55:55.96445459Z"}

TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal (internal/registry/resolve_test.go) asserts that when the project copy is absent but the global copy exists, ResolvePlugin returns the global path with layer==\"global\".\n3. Both tests use an isolated temp HOME/XDG fixture and do not touch the real user home.\n4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/registry/... green.\n5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"13eaa6a7758297f2001118d4533a33c70a272e9d","created_at":"2026-06-03T17:21:03.026972073Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 5 of epic ddx-602ae31a)\nPlugin content can live in two tiers — per-project (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/\u003cname\u003e) and machine-wide global (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e). Runtime resolution must deterministically prefer the project copy when present, fall back to the global copy, and finally to the baked-in default (ddx plugin only). Without a single authoritative resolver, install/update/init/doctor each reinvent path logic and drift.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- internal/registry/resolve.go:29 `ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name)` is the precedence resolver: project path computed at resolve.go:30, global at resolve.go:35, baked-in fallback at resolve.go:44. It must be the only place precedence is decided and must be covered by named tests so install/init/doctor can rely on it.\n- internal/registry/resolve.go:15 `GlobalPluginDir(name)` returns ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e via ddxroot.GlobalDir(); the project path routes through ddxroot.Path() (in-tree vs convention mode handled there).\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Add table-driven tests in internal/registry/resolve_test.go pinning both the prefers-project and falls-back-to-global behaviors against a temp HOME/XDG fixture.\n3. Do not alter install/update/init/doctor call sites here beyond confirming they call `ResolvePlugin` (those are covered by sibling children).\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Removing the per-project install tier (both tiers coexist).\n- Migrating ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.\n- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.\n- install/update/init/doctor wiring (sibling children).\n\nDEPS\nddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — ResolvePlugin routes through both ddxroot.Path() and ddxroot.GlobalDir(); both must be agreed-on before this lands.\n\nGOVERNING ARTIFACTS\n- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md (precedence decision record).\n- CLAUDE.md \"Persona System\" three-layer precedence note.","events_attachment":"ddx-3478153c/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-3478153c","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config","area:plugin","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-e6291b32","status":"closed","title":"registry: runtime plugin lookup precedence (project → global → baked-in)","updated_at":"2026-06-03T17:23:53.361549509Z","work-no-changes-count":1}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:130:{"acceptance":"1. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./... passes.\n2. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-06-07T02:17:59.120820292Z","description":"PROBLEM\nParent bead ddx-130cf9ab is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nPROBLEM\nParent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nThe decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:\n\n  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --\u003e child_A\n  e29892a4 -- parent --\u003e child_B\n  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           \u003c- BAD: parent is also a dep\n  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  \u003c- BAD\n\nThe parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.\n\nObserved scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):\n- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')\n- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)\n- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]\n- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores\n- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order\n\nRoot cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.\n\nThere is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nImplement only this child acceptance slice:\n- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.\n- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins asserts `ddx init` (no flag, in-project) creates \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/ and its manifest but does not write under plugins/ — the plugin lazy-resolves from the global tier.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... green.\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"4664c3d9dbff6177e63eb5dde486415159e2413f","created_at":"2026-06-03T05:42:23.055248099Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)\n\nThere is no first-time machine setup command, and `ddx init` bundles plugin install with manifest creation, forcing a plugin write into every newly initialized project instead of lazy-resolving from the global tier.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation; there is no `--global` umbrella path that installs the default `ddx` plugin globally and writes global config.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. `ddx init --global` is the umbrella first-time-setup command: installs the default `ddx` plugin globally to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (via GlobalPluginDir), creates agent-tier links (~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx pointing into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/), and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults.\n2. `ddx init` (no flag) creates \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/ and its manifest but does NOT install plugins; plugins are lazy-resolved on first use, preferring the project tier over global per the registry precedence bead.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Install command `--global` flag (sibling install bead).\n- Update command and doctor reporting (sibling update bead).\n- The runtime precedence implementation (registry bead).\n- Removing the existing per-project install (both modes coexist).\n- Migrating legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.\n- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.\n\nDEPS\n- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).\n- Registry resolution sibling bead (GlobalPluginDir) — init --global writes through it.\n\nGOVERNING ARTIFACTS\n- Epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2.","events_attachment":"ddx-3ecfd11d/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-3ecfd11d","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config","area:cli","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas","kind:feature","phase:3"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-b321fc9b","status":"closed","title":"init: add `ddx init --global` umbrella setup and make plain `ddx init` skip plugin install","updated_at":"2026-06-03T06:52:49.876373361Z"}

Current state of the foundational Store methods (cli/internal/bead/store.go): Init(args ...any) error                                  // line 149 GenID(args ...any) (string, error)                       // line 167 ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)                     // line 182 ReadAllFiltered(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)             // line 224 Create(args ...any) error                                // line 518 Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)                          // line 598 Update(args ...any) error                                // line 627 Close(args ...any) error                                 // line 1426 And in archive.go: GetWithArchive(id string) (*Bead, error)                 // line 172 Each variadic method extracts ctx via storeCallContext(args) and accepts either `s.Init()` or `s.Init(context.Background())` today. 2. AC1 requires these methods to take `ctx context.Context` as a typed first parameter (matching the BeadEventReader / BeadQueries shape already in cli/internal/bead/backend.go:91-114). Any concrete signature satisfying AC1 — `Init(ctx context.Context) error`, `Init(ctx context.Context, args ...any) error`, etc. — rejects the current zero-arg `s.Init()` callsites that exist in external packages. 3. The bead's PROPOSED FIX and NON-SCOPE explicitly forbid touching external callers (\"Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead\"; \"NON-SCOPE: Caller migration in cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/escalation, and metrics packages.\") and AC4 requires \"no caller-package changes in this bead.\" 4. AC3 requires `lefthook run pre-commit` to pass, which runs `go build -buildvcs=false -v ./...` from cli/ (lefthook.yml:122-129). That build covers every package in the module, including all the external caller packages NON-SCOPE forbids touching. 5. Concrete external breakage if AC1 is implemented today (`grep -rE '\\b(store|s)\\.(Init|Create|Update|Get|Close|ReadAll|GenID|GetWithArchive|ReadAllFiltered)\\(' cli/ --include='*.go'`): - 174 Go files reference these Store methods. - 73 distinct files outside cli/internal/bead/ use the zero-arg `store.Init()` form (e.g. cli/cmd/ac_test.go:37, cli/cmd/bead_human_test.go:20, cli/internal/agent/..., cli/internal/server/...). - Production-code stragglers without ctx exist too: cli/cmd/bead.go:414  s.GetWithArchive(args[0]) cli/cmd/bead.go:1281 s.Close(id) cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4485 store.Get(id) cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4811 store.Update(beadID, ...) `go build ./...` and the lefthook go-build hook will fail at every one of those callsites the instant Init/Get/Update/Close/ReadAll/GenID/ GetWithArchive/ReadAllFiltered become non-variadic ctx-first. There is no signature shape that satisfies AC1 while preserving all current call patterns (Go does not allow same-name method overloads on a single receiver type). 6. Step 0 size envelope is also exceeded for the only resolution that preserves AC1+AC3: the necessary mechanical caller sweep crosses ~170 files in five packages (cmd, internal/agent, internal/server, internal/attemptmetrics, internal/agentmetrics) — well past the \"~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages\" guardrail. That is exactly the sweep that NON-SCOPE excludes, and the parent split (TD-027 / ddx-464feba2) was rejected for the same reason. Operator action requested: Re-scope this bead to one of: (a) Add the caller-package migration to scope (delete the NON-SCOPE block and AC4's \"no caller-package changes\"). This becomes the original TD-027 sweep that previously failed as too large; if pursued, expect a multi-file diff across cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/attemptmetrics, cli/internal/agentmetrics. (b) Replace AC3 with a narrower verification (e.g. \"go test ./internal/bead/... and go vet ./internal/bead/... pass\"), and explicitly accept that `go build ./...` and lefthook pre-commit will be red until follow-up caller-migration beads land. The follow-up beads would then re-establish AC3 once the sweep is complete. (c) Replace AC1 with an additive shape that does not rename or retype the existing variadic methods — e.g. \"add new ctx-first Store methods with distinct names (InitCtx / GetCtx / …) so the legacy variadic surface stays in place during the migration\", then a follow-up bead deletes the variadic methods after the caller sweep finishes. This satisfies the spirit of TD-027 (introducing the typed ctx-first surface in the bead package first) without breaking the build outside the package. No code changes were made on this attempt. Request was triaged strictly against the bead body — no /tmp scratch files, no work outside the worktree.","work-last-status":"no_changes_operator_required","work-no-changes-count":1,"work-suggested-action":"review and accept, split, block, or cancel this proposed work"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:145:{"acceptance":"1. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... -run TestDispatchLogsErrorOnRootMutationWithBackEdge` passes.\n2. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/...` passes.\n3. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"236c09fd8b0f72a4b1e923a8e60aa03554e9395f","created_at":"2026-06-06T03:14:59.805964623Z","description":"PROBLEM\nThe lifecycle dispatch path still returns the generic `project root mutation rejected` error and the caller path continues to log it as a warning/continuing condition. When that happens in the same run that already exhibits a parent-back-edge state, the operator only sees a soft warning instead of the structural failure that actually occurred.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n`cli/internal/agent/lifecycle_dispatch.go:130-165` rejects root mutations but does not include the offending bead ID in the failure shape, and `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:595` plus the surrounding loop code consume the error as a warning path. The result is a muted signal even though the run is already in a bad structural state.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nWhen the parent-back-edge state and a root-mutation rejection coincide, upgrade the log/event severity from warning to error and attach the offending bead ID to the emitted message. Add the regression `TestDispatchLogsErrorOnRootMutationWithBackEdge` so the severity change cannot drift.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not change the store validator, doctor scan/repair, or decomposition prompt text in this bead.\n\nIn-scope files:\n- cli/internal/agent/lifecycle_dispatch.go\n- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go\n- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook_test.go\n\nOut-of-scope:\n- cli/internal/bead/store.go\n- cli/internal/bead/doctor.go\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go\n- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md","events_attachment":"ddx-4173d55c/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-4173d55c","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:tracker","area:lifecycle","area:decomposition","kind:safety","kind:correctness","ac-quality:needs-refinement"],"parent":"ddx-e19185aa","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-a9021df4","status":"closed","title":"Escalate lifecycle root-mutation rejection with back-edge context","updated_at":"2026-06-07T01:37:16.817994708Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:147:{"acceptance":"1. Add `TestExecuteBeadWorkerCannotMutatePrimaryGitConfig` or equivalent, using a real primary repo plus execute-bead worktree, that contaminates the worker attempt with commands such as `git config --local core.bare true`, `git config --local user.email fixture@ddx.test`, and a nested test subprocess with Git-local env vars set.\n2. The test asserts after the attempt that the primary checkout satisfies `git status --short`, has no `core.bare`, has no stray `core.worktree`, and does not contain fixture `user.name`/`user.email`.\n3. The fix ensures linked worktree attempts have per-worktree config isolation and/or scrubbed Git env for all agent/test subprocesses that DDx launches.\n4. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... ./internal/git/... -run \"TestExecuteBeadWorkerCannotMutatePrimaryGitConfig|TestGitFixtureHelpersStripHookEnv|TestCheckGitRepoHealthDetectsCoreBare\" -count=1` passes.\n5. `sh scripts/git-config-health.sh` passes after the new regression runs.\n6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","created_at":"2026-05-15T05:40:54.944068779Z","description":"PROBLEM\nDuring live `ddx work --watch --harness codex --model gpt-5.4` on bead `ddx-fa6eedcc` at 2026-05-15 05:38 ET, the primary checkout `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx` flipped to `core.bare=true`. `git status --short` in the primary checkout failed with `fatal: this operation must be run in a work tree`; `git config --local --get core.bare` returned `true`. I repaired the local checkout with `git config --unset-all --local core.bare`, but worker attempts must not be able to corrupt the operator's primary repo config.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\n`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:391-396` creates linked worktrees with `git worktree add --detach`; `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1242-1247` launches the worker in that isolated worktree. Doctor knows the corruption class at `cli/cmd/doctor.go:1035-1044` (`extensions.worktreeConfig` disabled can corrupt shared config), but this incident occurred while the main repo had `extensions.worktreeConfig=true`, so the execute-bead isolation contract still lacks an end-to-end guard that worker commands and tests cannot mutate the primary checkout's `.git/config` or `.git/config.worktree`.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nAdd an execute-bead regression that snapshots the primary checkout git config before a worker attempt, runs a script/agent attempt that intentionally tries to set `core.bare=true` and fixture identity from inside the attempt worktree and test subprocesses, and proves the primary checkout remains a valid worktree with no `core.bare`, no stray `core.worktree`, and no leaked fixture user config after the attempt. Harden the worker environment or worktree setup as needed.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not weaken git-config-health hooks. Do not hide the failure by suppressing `git status` errors. Do not rewrite execute-bead history or skip worker isolation; the fix must preserve isolated worktree execution while protecting the primary checkout.","id":"ddx-42a4819d","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:git","area:execution","kind:bug","operator-ux","dirty-tree"],"priority":0,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: prevent worker attempts from mutating primary git config","updated_at":"2026-05-15T11:57:09.098109835Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:148:{"acceptance":"1. `TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins` verifies that `ddx init` with no flag creates `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/` and the manifest but does not write `.ddx/plugins/ddx`, `.agents/skills/ddx`, `.claude/skills/ddx`, or any project-local `plugins/` tree.\n2. `TestInitProject_LeavesPluginsForLazyResolution` verifies the initialized project can lazy-resolve the default `ddx` plugin from the global layer through `registry.ResolvePlugin` when the sibling resolver behavior is present.\n3. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... -run 'TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins|TestInitProject_LeavesPluginsForLazyResolution'` passes.\n4. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/...` passes.\n5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"3b82c8ae41c24296760ec4c7b1bd41c7bb964c8b","created_at":"2026-06-02T03:19:04.472608585Z","description":"PROBLEM\nPlain `ddx init` eagerly installs the default `ddx` plugin into every project. The intended model is that project init only creates `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/` and the project manifest; plugins should lazy-resolve from the global layer on first use.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/cmd/init.go:73 `runInit` currently routes plain init through eager plugin setup.\n- cli/cmd/init.go:271-284 calls `registry.InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, defaultplugin.FS(), workingDir)` unconditionally, producing `.ddx/plugins/ddx`, `.agents/skills/ddx`, and `.claude/skills/ddx` in the project.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Remove the default plugin install from the no-flag `ddx init` path.\n2. Keep project-local initialization of `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/` and the manifest intact.\n3. Ensure the no-flag path does not create or modify any project `plugins/` tree or agent-tier skill links.\n4.
