.ddx/beads.jsonl:151:{"acceptance":"1. A new Store.Release (or equivalently named method) transitions a claimed bead to its target status (open by default), clears Owner, WriteAll, and removes the claim-heartbeat lease sidecar in a single WithLock critical section, mirroring Store.Claim. Verified by TestStoreReleaseClearsClaimAtomically in cli/internal/bead/store_test.go.\n2. After release, ddx bead status reports the bead as not in_progress with no owner and no lease sidecar remains. Verified by TestStoreReleaseClearsClaimAtomically.\n3. A concurrent (multi-goroutine/process) test inspects status mid-claim: while claimed the bead reports in_progress with an owner; after release it reports open with no owner. Verified by TestClaimReleaseStatusReflectsLease in cli/internal/bead/store_test.go.\n4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/bead/... green\n5. lefthook run pre-commit passes","closing_commit_sha":"2062dcca300c900419ffbdc97772bf4982b67afa","created_at":"2026-05-26T18:24:48.686281455Z","description":"PROBLEM\nWhen a worker abandons a bead (route-resolution timeout, watchdog fire, or any failure release), the bead's claim/lease must be cleared atomically and `ddx bead status` must reflect the lease state. Claiming a bead today atomically transitions it to in_progress, but there is no symmetric release path that, in a single locked write, transitions the bead back and clears the lease. The parent bug (ddx-8f2e0ebf) observed \"bead has a claim but ddx bead status shows In progress: 0\" — claim and lifecycle status not atomically coupled on the release side.\n\nROOT CAUSE\ncli/internal/bead/store.go: Store.Claim (~lines 863-930) is atomic — under WithLock it calls transitionLifecycleInPlace(StatusInProgress) + sets Owner + WriteAll, then writeClaimHeartbeat writes the lease sidecar. There is NO symmetric Store.Release that, under the same WithLock, transitions status back (default open), clears Owner, WriteAll, AND removes the claim-heartbeat lease sidecar in one critical section. Failure-release paths can leave a stale lease sidecar while status reports not-in_progress, producing the phantom-lease inconsistency.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Add Store.Release(id, assignee, targetStatus) mirroring Store.Claim: under WithLock, read beads, transitionLifecycleInPlace to target status (open by default), clear Owner, WriteAll, and remove the claim-heartbeat lease sidecar — all in one locked operation.\n2. Route the worker's failure-release paths through Store.Release so claim clearing and status are coupled.\n3. Ensure `ddx bead status` counts reflect lease state: a released bead is not counted in_progress and has no owner; a claimed bead is in_progress with an owner.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nRoute-resolution timeout (sibling child A), heartbeat watchdog (child B), same-bead-twice guard (child D), operator-attention query surface + docs (child E). This bead only establishes atomic claim/release symmetry and status accuracy. This is a decomposition child of ddx-8f2e0ebf.","events_attachment":"ddx-449baa1d/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-449baa1d","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:workers","area:routing","kind:bug","kind:reliability","observability","priority:reliability"],"parent":"ddx-8f2e0ebf","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-ca25ffb9","status":"closed","title":"bead: add atomic Store.Release so failure-release clears the claim and status reflects the lease","updated_at":"2026-05-26T18:33:43.642197008Z"}

Observed on 2026-05-15 in `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau`: readiness/decomposition for `fizeau-930b498b` dispatched `MODE: intake` / `MODE: preclaim-decompose` with the request workdir set to the Fizeau project root, and the main checkout was left dirty with `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `internal/routehealth/probe_store.go`, `website/benchmark_responsive_test.go`, and a newly-created release-blocker bead (`fizeau-b5b6dff2`). Lifecycle calls are supposed to classify, rewrite, or propose children; they must not have a writable project-root execution context.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\n`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:381-395` builds an `AgentRunRuntime` with `WorkDir: projectRoot` and dispatches the readiness model call through the normal service path.\n`cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:60-70` does the same for pre-claim decomposition, and `cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:150-161` does the same for post-ladder decomposition.\n`cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:172-176` turns an empty runtime workdir into the project workdir, and `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:244-252` sends that workdir to Fizeau as `ServiceExecuteRequest.WorkDir`. With normal project permissions, Codex/Claude/Gemini lifecycle calls can edit files in the checkout even though DDx only needs their JSON response.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nRun all hidden lifecycle model calls (pre-claim intake, pre-claim decomposition, and post-ladder decomposition) in a DDx-owned scratch execution directory or otherwise enforce a read-only/no-write dispatch boundary. Keep reading bead/project context through DDx code, parse the model JSON response, and apply any tracker mutations through DDx store APIs after validation. Add a post-dispatch guard that fails the lifecycle call if the project root becomes dirty from anything other than DDx-owned tracker mutations performed by the orchestrator itself.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not change normal `ddx try` / execute-bead worktree execution. Do not let lifecycle agents commit code or edit project files. Do not weaken decomposition output validation or tracker API usage.","events_attachment":"ddx-492050f3/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-492050f3","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:execution","area:agent","area:work","kind:bug","dirty-tree","principle:isolation"],"priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-f4aa254d","status":"closed","title":"work: isolate lifecycle model calls from project root","updated_at":"2026-05-15T16:36:24.296964643Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:157:{"acceptance":"1. TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorktrees asserts that with N pre-existing /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-* worktrees whose owning processes are dead and whose mtime is older than DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE, `ddx work --once` (cold start) removes them via git worktree remove + rm -rf before claiming any bead.\n2. TestWorkStartup_PreservesLiveWorktrees asserts that worktrees whose owning ddx-try/ddx-work process is still alive are NOT reaped.\n3. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","claimed-at":"2026-05-15T06:09:55Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"4026275","closing_commit_sha":"0a6f064c2e4c34b74e5cf00eecb8b49efe3eaac4","created_at":"2026-05-15T02:42:34.077865828Z","description":"PROBLEM\nParent bead ddx-c191e91a is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nPROBLEM\nDDx-managed filesystem state accumulates indefinitely. On the Fizeau project (sibling repo, /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau) on 2026-05-14:\n- /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/ contained 9,735 inodes across ~9700+ directories. Many were .execute-bead-wt-* worktrees dating back to 2026-05-07 (a week stale). Each is a full repo checkout, multiplying repo size on disk.\n- .ddx/workers/ held 35 agent-loop-\u003cnano-id\u003e/ directories. Most show last_activity_at older than 1 hour with no live process. They keep persisting per-attempt status.json entries.\n- .ddx/executions/ held 44 attempt directories. Some have unmerged result.json state (a previous merge or worker crash left them in DU). No automatic GC.\n- .ddx/attachments/ holds 355 per-bead artifact directories. They flood `git status` as untracked since nothing gitignores them.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- `ddx try` creates a worktree under `/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-\u003cbead\u003e-\u003ctimestamp\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/` and only sometimes cleans up. Failure paths (worktree_lost, execution_failed, process crash) consistently leave the worktree behind. There's no startup reaper for stale worktrees.\n- `ddx work` and `ddx try` write a worker status.json under .ddx/workers/agent-loop-\u003cnano-id\u003e/ and the writer is responsible for cleanup. Process crashes leave the directory behind; no startup reaper claims them.\n- .ddx/executions/\u003ctimestamp\u003e/ is meant to be retained as evidence (per CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010 evidence pile). But there's no retention policy: directories accumulate forever. Some land in unmerged git state after partial commits.\n- .ddx/attachments/ and .ddx/run-state*.json are written by every ddx invocation, but the project's .gitignore policy for them is inconsistent (.ddx/executions is ignored; .ddx/attachments and .ddx/run-state.json appear in `git status` constantly).\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Add a startup reaper to `ddx work` and `ddx try`: on launch, scan /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/, identify worktrees whose parent ddx-try/ddx-work process is dead (no PID match), `git worktree remove` + `rm -rf` them. Bound the scan to N=200 per launch so a cold start doesn't take forever. Configurable via DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE (default 72h).\n2. Add a startup reaper for `.ddx/workers/agent-loop-*/`: read status.json's `pid` and `last_activity_at`; if pid is gone OR activity \u003e 30 min stale, remove the directory.\n3. .ddx/beads.jsonl:164:{"acceptance":"1. TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks asserts ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx point into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/.\n2. TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins asserts `ddx init` (in-project) does not write to plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve from global.\n3. A Test* in cli/cmd/doctor_test.go asserts doctor renders both the \"Global Install (...)\" and \"Project Install (...)\" lines with the correct ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global statuses (doctor_test.go:273,276).\n4. A test asserts the ddx-validate pre-commit hook runs skill validation only when skill files are staged, independent of install location.\n5. The SKILL.md \"How this skill works\" install-locations note is updated in this bead's commit.\n6. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... green.\n7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"bd11f3c9adebedc32b3639a97362f4129071bab6","created_at":"2026-06-03T01:02:41.360272672Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)\n\nFirst-time machine setup needs an umbrella `ddx init --global` that installs the default ddx plugin globally and wires agent-tier links, while plain `ddx init` must create only the project workspace and lazy-resolve plugins (preferring project over global) rather than eagerly installing. `ddx doctor` must surface both install layers so operators can see where the skill resolves from. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook (ddx-777f4a09, already landed) must keep scoping skill checks to staged skill files regardless of install location.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/cmd/init.go:75 dispatches to initGlobal (init.go:318) when --global is set; initGlobal installs the bundled default ddx plugin into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (init.go:320), creates symlinks ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx into that dir (init.go:336), and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults (init.go:351, writeGlobalConfig init.go:362).\n- Plain `ddx init` (no flag) must create \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/ and NOT write plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve via the registry resolver, preferring the project copy over global.\n- cli/cmd/doctor.go:421-448 computes globalPath/projectPath and the projectStatus \"lazy-resolves-to-global\" (doctor.go:436-444) and prints the \"Global Install (...) — \u003cstatus\u003e\" and \"Project Install (...) — \u003cstatus\u003e\" lines (doctor.go:447-448).\n- Before this, `ddx init` bundled plugin install with manifest creation, there was no global setup command, and doctor reported only a single install layer.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. `ddx init --global` installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx links into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx, and writes the global config with convention defaults.\n2. `ddx init` (no flag) creates \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/ and does NOT install plugins; first use lazy-resolves from the project layer, falling back to global.\n3. `ddx doctor` renders two lines: \"Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok|missing\" and \"Project Install (\u003cprojectRoot\u003e/plugins/ddx) — ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global\".\n4. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook continues to run skill-related validation only when skill files are staged, regardless of whether the install is global or project-scoped.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Does NOT remove the per-project install path (both layers coexist).\n- Does NOT migrate legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.\n- Does NOT add agent-harness skill paths beyond Claude Code and Codex.\n- Plugin content writing for non-init installs is owned by the install child beads.\n\nDEPS\nddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — global setup routes through ddxroot.GlobalDir(), project init through ddxroot.Path(). Governing epic: ddx-602ae31a (Principle 2). Related: ddx-777f4a09 (ddx-validate hook, landed).","events_attachment":"ddx-4c58ea3e/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-4c58ea3e","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:cli","area:config","area:plugin","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-04beccf3","status":"closed","title":"init/doctor: --global umbrella setup, lazy project init, and two-layer doctor reporting","updated_at":"2026-06-03T02:46:46.787576605Z"}

Suggests this decoder bug appeared mid-session OR only affects certain readiness routes.\n\nWHY THIS IS A BUG\n1. Schema drift between the readiness-check producer and the pre_claim_intake consumer. One emits verdict as bool, the other parses it as string. Self-contained.","events_attachment":"ddx-4ef3e71a/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-4ef3e71a","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:workers","kind:reliability","priority:reliability","phase:0","decomposed","triage:no-changes-unjustified"],"parent":"ddx-2a24727c","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-41e88604","status":"closed","title":"ddx work: each attempt runs in an isolated worktree; failure reverts/cleans before next attempt","updated_at":"2026-06-01T02:38:39.154586303Z","work-no-changes-count":2}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:175:{"acceptance":"See bead body — 5 verifiable AC items: new ddxroot regression test, ~20 test fixtures updated to write config.yaml, go test -short ./internal/... green, orphan-warning at startup, pre-commit clean.","created_at":"2026-06-01T21:09:49.582933254Z","description":"**The bug**\n\n`cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:Path()` resolves a project's state root in\none of two locations based on whether `\u003cprojectRoot\u003e/.ddx/` exists as a\ndirectory:\n\n```go\nfunc Path(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string) string {\n    inTree := InTree(projectRoot)\n    if info, err := os.Stat(inTree); err == nil \u0026\u0026 info.IsDir() {\n        return inTree    // \u003cprojectRoot\u003e/.ddx/\n    }\n    root := filepath.Join(projectsRoot(), projectIdentity(ctx, projectRoot))\n    _ = bootstrapConventionRoot(ctx, projectRoot, root)\n    return root          // ~/.local/share/ddx/projects/\u003cidentity\u003e\n}\n```\n\nThe `.ddx/` directory test is too weak. It accepts ANY directory named\n`.ddx/` as evidence that `projectRoot` is a DDx project. The home\ndirectory `$HOME` happens to have a `~/.ddx/` directory because that's\nwhere user-global state (`installed.yaml`, `plugins/`) lives via the\nparallel `JoinHome()` helper. So if any `ddx ...` invocation is made\nwith `projectRoot=$HOME` (e.g., via `cwd=$HOME` and no `--project`\noverride), `Path()` returns `~/.ddx/` as if the user's home were a\nproject root.\n\nResult: the same logical state files appear at two different paths\ndepending on how the binary was invoked, and the duplicates drift.\nThe concrete instance that triggered this investigation was the TLS\ncert: `ddx server` running with `WorkingDir=/home/erik/Projects/ddx`\n(via systemd) wrote its cert at\n`/home/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/server/tls/cert.pem`, while a separate\ninvocation (probably manual, from `$HOME`) wrote\n`/home/erik/.ddx/server/tls/cert.pem` — different fingerprint, same\nfilename, same purpose. The server served the project-root cert; some\nother client trusted the home-dir cert; every connection got\n`tls: bad certificate`.\n\n**Audit — every consumer of the dual-path resolver**\n\n`ddxroot.JoinProject` / `ddxroot.Path` is called from ~50 callsites\nacross the codebase. Every one of them inherits the bifurcation. The\nhighest-risk consumers (those that read or write state that must NOT\ndiverge between locations):\n\n- `beads.jsonl` (`execute_bead.go:2165`, `execute_bead.go:2722`,\n  `execute_bead_dangling_success.go:189`, `agentmetrics/loader.go:244`,\n  `service_run.go:396`, `server.go:797`, `server.go:5043`, `exec/store.go:56`)\n- `metrics/locks.jsonl` (`lockmetrics/lockmetrics.go:96`)\n- `metrics/attempts.jsonl` (`attemptmetrics/attemptmetrics.go:99`)\n- `.git-tracker.lock` (`lockmetrics/lockcap.go:221`)\n- `server/tls/cert.pem` + `key.pem` (`server/server.go:326`)\n- `tsnet/` (`server/server.go:503`)\n- `workers/\u003cid\u003e/` (`execution_cleanup.go:1370`, `workerstatus/liveness.go:44`,\n  `executions_mirror.go:211`)\n- `executions/\u003cattempt-id\u003e/` (`prose_evidence.go:125`,\n  `reviewer_stream_artifact.go:41,45`, `agentmetrics/loader.go:23,27`)\n- `agent-logs/` (`execution_cleanup.go:1268`)\n- `config.yaml` (`init.go:155`, `execution_cleanup.go:221`,\n  `execution_worktree_root.go:23`)\n- `exec/definitions/`, `exec/runs/` (`exec/path_helpers.go:14,18`)\n- `graphs/` (`docgraph.go:1197`)\n- `run-state.json` + `RunStateDir` (`agent/run_state.go:60,64`)\n- `tracker_lock.go:116` (the bead tracker lock)\n\nAnything in this list reachable from two different `projectRoot`\narguments (or with one of them being `$HOME`) ends up duplicated.\n\n**Proposed fix (structural)**\n\nTighten the in-tree check. A bare `.ddx/` directory is necessary but\nnot sufficient evidence of a project root. Require an initialization\nmarker: `\u003cprojectRoot\u003e/.ddx/config.yaml` (the file written by\n`ddx init` at `cli/cmd/init.go:236`). Patch:\n\n```go\nfunc Path(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string) string {\n    inTree := InTree(projectRoot)\n    if isInitializedInTree(inTree) {   // was: os.Stat / IsDir\n        return inTree\n    }\n    root := filepath.Join(projectsRoot(), projectIdentity(ctx, projectRoot))\n    _ = bootstrapConventionRoot(ctx, projectRoot, root)\n    return root\n}\n\nfunc isInitializedInTree(dir string) bool {\n    info, err := os.Stat(dir)\n    if err != nil || !info.IsDir() {\n        return false\n    }\n    _, err = os.Stat(filepath.Join(dir, \"config.yaml\"))\n    return err == nil\n}\n```\n\n`ExistingPath` gets the same treatment.\n\nThis makes `Path($HOME)` correctly fall through to\n`~/.local/share/ddx/projects/\u003cidentity\u003e` (since `~/.ddx/` has no\n`config.yaml`), eliminating the dual-location bifurcation entirely.\n\n**Blast radius (this is what blocks landing the patch as-is)**\n\nA surveyed initial spike against this patch made `internal/ddxroot`\ntests pass, but exposed ~20 test fixtures elsewhere that build a bare\n`.ddx/` directory and rely on the old \"any `.ddx/` = project root\"\ncontract. Failing packages from a quick `go test -short ./internal/...`\nrun:\n\n- `internal/server/graphql` (9 failures: `TestAgentMetrics_*`,\n  `TestNode_ResolvesBeadByID_ScopedToProject`, `TestNode_ResolvesExecRunByID_ScopedToProject`)\n\nFiles that need their test fixtures updated to also write a `config.yaml`\nin the `.ddx/`:\n\n- `internal/server/graphql/resolver_agent_metrics_test.go` (fixtureWorkingDir)\n- `internal/server/graphql/resolver_workingdir_ctx_test.go`\n- `internal/agent/execution_cleanup_test.go` (3 callsites)\n- `internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_interrupted_attempt_test.go` (2 callsites)\n- `internal/agent/execute_bead_commit_test.go`\n- `internal/agent/execute_bead_worktree_cleanup_test.go`\n- `internal/agent/serviceconfig_test.go`\n- `internal/agent/drain_regression_test.go`\n- `internal/agent/operator_prompt_backlinks_test.go`\n- `internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go` (6 callsites)\n- `internal/agent/execute_bead_artifacts_test.go`\n- `internal/agent/execution_resources_test.go` (3 callsites)\n\nA central helper (e.g., `testutils.MakeInitializedDDxRoot(t, dir)`)\nthat creates `.ddx/` + writes a stub `config.yaml` would cap the diff.\n\n**Migration**\n\nExisting user-global state at `~/.ddx/server/tls/`, `~/.ddx/tsnet/` etc.\ngets orphaned by the fix (they were created when `Path($HOME)`\nreturned `~/.ddx/`; afterwards Path uses the XDG path). The transition\nis graceful for TLS (server regenerates a fresh self-signed cert) but\noperators with manual config in those subdirs will need a one-shot\nmigration. Recommend:\n\n1. Emit a startup warning when `~/.ddx/server/` exists alongside the\n   XDG path, telling the operator to delete the orphan.\n2. Or auto-move `~/.ddx/server/` → `~/.local/share/ddx/projects/\u003chost-id\u003e/server/`\n   on first server start under the new resolver.\n\n**Operational note**\n\nThe original sindri-dev TLS bad-cert storm (~36 hours, blocking the\nentire ddx work queue) was unblocked by manually copying the home-dir\ncert+key over the project-root location and restarting ddx-server.\nThat's a band-aid; this bead is the proper unwind.\n\n**Severity: P1**\n\nThe bug is latent across most state files. The reason it manifested as\n\"server unreachable\" rather than \"bead tracker corrupted\" is that\nnobody was running ddx commands as `$HOME` against beads files\nrecently. Any operator who does (or any future feature that uses\n`projectRoot=$HOME` for any reason) will silently bifurcate state.\n\n**Acceptance**\n\n1. `go test ./internal/ddxroot/...` includes a new regression test\n   (`TestDDxRoot_BareInTreeFallsThroughToXDG`) that verifies a `.ddx/`\n   without `config.yaml` falls through to the XDG path.\n2. All 20+ test fixtures listed above updated to write\n   `.ddx/config.yaml` (or use the new testutils helper).\n3. `go test -short ./internal/...` passes.\n4. Startup or migration logic warns about orphan `~/.ddx/server/` /\n   `~/.ddx/tsnet/` directories.\n5. Pre-commit / lefthook green.\n\n**Out of scope**\n\n- Renaming `~/.ddx/` to `~/.config/ddx/` (separate cleanup).\n- Multi-project federation collision (different concern, different\n  layer).\n- The TLS-renew schedule / rotation logic (orthogonal — the bug is\n  not \"the cert was rotated\"; it's \"two carts exist\").","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"{\"ac_map\":[{\"parent_ac\":\"1. `go test ./internal/ddxroot/...` includes a new regression test (`TestDDxRoot_BareInTreeFallsThroughToXDG`) that verifies a `.ddx/` without `config.yaml` falls through to the XDG path.\",\"coverage\":\"Child 1 AC 1 covers `TestDDxRoot_BareInTreeFallsThroughToXDG`; Child 1 AC 4 covers `cd cli \\u0026\\u0026 go test ./internal/ddxroot/...`.\"},{\"parent_ac\":\"2. All 20+ test fixtures listed above updated to write `.ddx/config.yaml` (or use the new testutils helper).\",\"coverage\":\"Child 2 AC 1 covers the optional helper; Child 2 AC 2 covers `internal/server/graphql`; Child 2 AC 3 covers the listed `internal/agent` fixture files.\"},{\"parent_ac\":\"3. `go test -short ./internal/...` passes.\",\"coverage\":\"Child 2 AC 5 covers `cd cli \\u0026\\u0026 go test -short ./internal/...` after combining with the resolver change; Child 1 AC 4 and Child 3 AC 4 provide package-local gates.\"},{\"parent_ac\":\"4. Startup or migration logic warns about orphan `~/.ddx/server/` / `~/.ddx/tsnet/` directories.\",\"coverage\":\"Child 3 AC 1 covers orphan `~/.ddx/server/`; Child 3 AC 2 covers orphan `~/.ddx/tsnet/`; Child 3 AC 3 covers operator-useful warning text.\"},{\"parent_ac\":\"5. Pre-commit / lefthook green.\",\"coverage\":\"Child 1 AC 5, Child 2 AC 6, and Child 3 AC 5 each require `lefthook run pre-commit`.\"}],\"child_ids\":[\"ddx-36e865f1\",\"ddx-e630e11d\",\"ddx-4ee830ff\"],\"rationale\":\"Split into three independently executable units: resolver semantics and focused ddxroot regression coverage, cross-package fixture modernization needed by the new resolver contract, and server-facing orphan-state operator warning logic. This keeps production behavior, test blast-radius cleanup, and migration/operator messaging separate while preserving all parent non-scope items and verification requirements.\"}","created_at":"2026-06-07T08:42:14.438252273Z","kind":"triage-decomposed","source":"ddx work","summary":"decomposed into ddx-36e865f1, ddx-e630e11d, ddx-4ee830ff"}],"execution-eligible":false,"id":"ddx-5014f260","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:state","area:server","kind:bug","kind:reliability","regression"],"priority":1,"schema_version":1,"status":"open","title":"ddx state root: tighten Path() in-tree check to require config.yaml so user-global ~/.ddx/ doesn't shadow XDG project root","updated_at":"2026-06-07T08:42:15.175259147Z","work-last-detail":"tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/.git-tracker.lock, owner pid: 1993005)","work-last-status":"preclaim-hook-failed","work-retry-after":"2026-06-06T15:36:00Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:179:{"acceptance":"1. TestWorkLoop_IdlePassEpicClosureIsIdempotent: a second idle pass closes zero additional epics and does not error.\n3. TestWorkLoop_EpicWithOpenChildNotClosedByIdlePass: an epic with one open child is not closed.\n4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TestWorkLoop_.*Epic' green.\n5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"fe5c06c06bfce323eceb5fedd714053ddbcad7cb","created_at":"2026-06-05T22:06:45.072804498Z","description":"PROBLEM: FEAT-004 §Queue Semantics For Epics (docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:134-136,158-161) requires the ddx work idle-path remediator to fire a \"closure cascade\" auto-action that closes epics whose children are all terminal — and forbids the loop from skipping/exiting while ready epics remain. Today the loop only REPORTS EpicClosureCandidates (internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3432,3461; cmd/work.go:345) and SKIPS them as epic_container (execute_bead_loop.go:3694-3695). The recovery hook (internal/agent/recovery_hook.go:71-94) dispatches reframe/decompose/park but has NO closure action. So an all-children-closed epic is reported and skipped, never closed — a FEAT-010 reliability-contract violation.\n\nROOT CAUSE: no idle-path step invokes the closure path; recovery_hook.go lacks a closure-cascade remediation.\n\nPROPOSED FIX: Add an idle-path remediation step (before declaring \"no execution-eligible beads\") that calls Store.EpicClosureCandidates(ctx) (store.go:1702) and closes each via Store.Close (which already emits the epic_auto_close audit event and runs the walk-up, including the cancelled-terminal + all-cancelled-cancels refinement from the CloseWithEvidence bead). Idempotent: closed epics drop out of EpicClosureCandidates so a second pass closes nothing new. Record closures in the work-log snapshot (reuse EpicClosureCandidatesCount, execute_bead_loop.go:3461).\n\nNON-SCOPE: the CloseWithEvidence hook (separate bead); reconcile; recursion.","events_attachment":"ddx-514f36d1/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-514f36d1","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","kind:bug","kind:reliability","phase:iterate","spec:FEAT-004","epic-autoclose"],"priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-03e0a3c2","status":"closed","title":"epic-autoclose: add idle-path closure-cascade remediation to ddx work","updated_at":"2026-06-06T07:16:29.80231001Z"}

Without a diagnostic and repair path, those trackers remain wedged even after future writers stop producing bad edges.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: `ddx bead doctor` is wired in `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go:31` and delegates normal scan/fix to `bead.BeadDoctor` / `bead.BeadDoctorFix` at `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go:110`, but `cli/internal/bead/doctor.go:34` only scans oversized fields. It never builds a parent graph or reports dependency edges that intersect the parent chain.\n\nPROPOSED FIX: Extend bead doctor with a `parent_ancestor_in_deps` check that scans all bead rows, walks each bead's parent chain, and reports any dependency target that equals the direct parent or any ancestor. JSON output must be stable and scriptable. `--fix` must remove only offending dependency edges, preserve unrelated deps and events, and remain idempotent. Keep existing oversized-field and dangling-success behavior intact.\n\nNON-SCOPE: Do not modify decomposition prompts. Do not change `ddx work` retry behavior. Do not manually edit tracker storage outside the doctor implementation. Do not remove valid `parent -\u003e child` dependencies.\n\nGOVERNING ARTIFACTS: `docs/triage/decomposition.md:75` makes AC mapping load-bearing; this diagnostic preserves existing child work without lossy tracker rewrites.\n\nINHERITED DEPENDENCIES: none specified by the parent bead.","events_attachment":"ddx-5255a25c/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-5255a25c","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:tracker","area:lifecycle","kind:safety","kind:correctness","ac-quality:needs-refinement"],"parent":"ddx-e19185aa","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-5cd7eadf","status":"closed","title":"Detect and repair parent ancestor deps in bead doctor","updated_at":"2026-06-07T04:12:26.084120204Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:184:{"acceptance":"1. TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal (cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go): with the plugin dir present under BOTH `\u003cprojectRoot\u003e/.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e` and `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e`, `ResolvePlugin` returns layer==\"project\" and the project path.\n2. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal (cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go): with the project copy absent and only the global copy present, `ResolvePlugin` returns layer==\"global\" and the path returned by `GlobalPluginDir(name)`.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/registry/... green.\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"ecb015c2fb2effc5bff40afd42cf058b2606c65e","created_at":"2026-06-03T07:17:39.015088022Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)\nRuntime plugin lookup must follow a single authoritative precedence — install-in-project (project tier) overrides install-to-agent (global tier), with the embedded default ddx plugin as the last-resort baked-in fallback. Without one resolver, install/update/doctor each reimplement path logic and drift, so a project-local plugin can be silently shadowed by a global copy (or vice-versa).\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:29 `ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name)` is the intended single entry point. It checks the project path `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/\u003cname\u003e` (resolve.go:30), then the global path `ddxroot.GlobalDir()/plugins/\u003cname\u003e` (resolve.go:35), then returns layer `\"baked-in\"` with an empty path only for name==\"ddx\" (resolve.go:41), else errors (resolve.go:44).\n- cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:15 `GlobalPluginDir(name)` defines the global-tier path `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e`.\n- The precedence contract is currently under-tested, so regressions in ordering would go undetected.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Guarantee `ResolvePlugin` returns layer `\"project\"` and the project path when the plugin dir exists in BOTH the project and global tiers (project wins).\n- Guarantee it returns layer `\"global\"` and the global path when the project copy is absent but the global copy exists.\n- Keep baked-in (empty path, caller uses `defaultplugin.FS()`) valid only for name==\"ddx\"; return an error for an unknown plugin present in no tier.\n- Add table-driven coverage in cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go using `t.Setenv(\"XDG_DATA_HOME\", ...)` and a temp projectRoot with an in-tree `.ddx/` so both tiers are exercised deterministically.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Changing how install/update/doctor WRITE plugins (covered by sibling beads).\n- Removing the existing per-project install (both modes coexist).\n- Migrating any legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.\n- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.\n\nDEPS\n- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution): `ResolvePlugin` routes through `ddxroot.Path()` and `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`; both must be agreed-on. No other deps — this bead is the foundation the install/update/doctor siblings build on.","events_attachment":"ddx-536c3dd2/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-536c3dd2","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:plugin","area:config","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-b67715d8","status":"closed","title":"registry: ResolvePlugin honors project→global→baked-in precedence with table tests","updated_at":"2026-06-03T07:21:01.247260548Z","work-no-changes-count":1}

None of the 23 ready beads got claimed in the prior 30 minutes; commit log empty for the same window.\n- Other beads (fizeau-a15106ab, fizeau-90b85c8b) WERE successfully closed earlier in the night through some other path (perhaps a different worker version or a different execute-bead invocation). One emits verdict as bool, the other parses it as string. Either the producer or consumer changed without coordination.\n2. warn-only policy_mode masks the failure: the worker keeps polling and looks alive (loop.idle every 50s) but never claims anything. Operator has no signal beyond tailing the agent-loop log.\n3. The 'suggested_action' text ('check the\n...[truncated]\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nImplement only this child acceptance slice:\n- CI schema gate: the readiness_checks JSON schema is defined in a shared place; producer and consumer both consume it; CI fails on schema drift.\n- Same-fingerprint escalation: after N (default 5) consecutive warn outcomes with the same fingerprint, the worker emits an operator-attention event and stops looping silently. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.\n2. 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./... passes.\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"2bbb93e87a3920ca453101fdeaea9565f382c185","created_at":"2026-06-06T03:22:30.475161508Z","description":"PROBLEM\nParent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nThe decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:\n\n  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --\u003e child_A\n  e29892a4 -- parent --\u003e child_B\n  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           \u003c- BAD: parent is also a dep\n  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  \u003c- BAD\n\nThe parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.\n\nObserved scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):\n- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')\n- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)\n- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]\n- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores\n- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order\n\nRoot cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.\n\nThere is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nImplement only this child acceptance slice:\n- 'ddx bead dep add \u003cbead\u003e \u003cdep\u003e' HARD-REJECTS the edge when \u003cdep\u003e is \u003cbead\u003e's parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.\n- 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.","events_attachment":"ddx-63780ef6/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-63780ef6","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:tracker","area:lifecycle","area:decomposition","kind:safety","kind:correctness","ac-quality:needs-refinement","decomposed"],"parent":"ddx-e19185aa","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-7e9e7432","status":"closed","title":"Reject bead dep edges that re-introduce the parent or any ancestor: part 1","updated_at":"2026-06-07T08:20:25.284955863Z","work-no-changes-count":4}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:210:{"acceptance":"1. `ddx init --global` creates `~/.claude/skills/ddx` and `~/.agents/skills/ddx` pointing into `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/` and writes `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml` — TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks.\n2. `ddx init` (no flag, in-project) does NOT write to any `plugins/` tree; the plugin lazy-resolves from the global layer — TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... green.\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"3946d29110f96ee9d380f6ad28f88ab1fa4fb526","created_at":"2026-06-02T01:50:49.178056222Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-64ecd687","depends_on_id":"ddx-07af62e6","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-06-02T03:19:04Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-64ecd687","depends_on_id":"ddx-42c2c630","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-06-02T03:19:04Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM\nFirst-time per-machine setup has no entry point, and `ddx init` eagerly installs the default ddx plugin into every project. The intended model is: `ddx init --global` does one-time machine setup (global default plugin + agent-tier links + global config), while plain `ddx init` only creates `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/` and lazily resolves plugins from the global layer on first use.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/cmd/init.go:73 `runInit` always installs the default ddx plugin (init.go:271-284 via `registry.InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, defaultplugin.FS(), workingDir)`), producing `.ddx/plugins/ddx`, `.agents/skills/ddx`, `.claude/skills/ddx` unconditionally.\n- There is no `--global` flag and no `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml` writer.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Add `--global` bool flag to `ddx init` (cli/cmd/init.go).\n2. init --global: install the default `ddx` plugin to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx` (via the install-global path from the sibling install bead), create agent-tier links `~/.claude/skills/ddx` and `~/.agents/skills/ddx` pointing into the global plugin dir, and write `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml` with convention defaults.\n3. init (no flag): create `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/` and the manifest, but do NOT install any plugin — plugins lazy-resolve via `registry.ResolvePlugin` (sibling resolver bead), preferring project over global, on first use. Remove the eager install at init.go:271-284 from the no-flag path.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- The resolver precedence itself (sibling bead: registry two-layer resolution).\n- install/update `--global` wiring (sibling bead).\n- `ddx doctor` reporting and spec/doc updates (sibling bead: doctor + specs).\n- Migrating any `~/.config/ddx/` legacy paths.\n\nDEPS\nddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution); sibling registry-resolver bead; sibling install/update --global bead (reuse its agent-tier link helper).","events_attachment":"ddx-64ecd687/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-64ecd687","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:plugin","area:cli","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-a08a425b","status":"closed","title":"init: add `ddx init --global` umbrella setup and make plain `ddx init` lazy-resolve plugins","updated_at":"2026-06-02T05:38:52.288275281Z","work-no-changes-count":1}

TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks (cli/cmd/init_test.go or init_functional_test.go) asserts `ddx init --global` makes ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx resolve into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/ and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml.\n2. TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins (cli/cmd/init_test.go) asserts plain `ddx init` creates `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/` and writes nothing under plugins/.\n3. TestDoctor_ReportsInstallTopology (cli/cmd/doctor_plugins_test.go) asserts `ddx doctor` output contains a \"Global Install (...) — ok|missing\" line and a \"Project Install (...) — ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global\" line, and that the project line reads `lazy-resolves-to-global` when only the global install is present.\n4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... green.\n5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"5843edc5ebcb5f64b14cae384b8f22dbb6bdbea5","created_at":"2026-06-03T12:37:10.50344695Z","description":"PROBLEM (epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2)\n\nFirst-time machine setup needs a single umbrella command (`ddx init --global`) that installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates the agent-tier links, and writes the global config. A plain `ddx init` must create the project workspace WITHOUT installing plugins (they lazy-resolve from the global/baked-in layers on first use). `ddx doctor` must surface both install layers so operators can see whether a project install exists or is lazy-resolving to global.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- `ddx init --global` dispatches to `initGlobal()` (cli/cmd/init.go:75,318), which: creates ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (init.go:319-323), installs the bundled default plugin there (init.go:328), creates agent-tier links so every harness discovers the skill (init.go:314-316), and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults via `writeGlobalConfig` (init.go:351-366).\n- Plain `ddx init` (no flag) must create `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/` and must NOT write to plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve via ResolvePlugin (project→global→baked-in).\n- `ddx doctor` topology reporting lives at cli/cmd/doctor.go:415 `reportSkillInstallTopology`, which prints `Global Install (\u003cglobalPath\u003e) — ok|missing` (doctor.go:447) and `Project Install (\u003cprojectPath\u003e) — ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global` (doctor.go:448), using `registry.ResolvePlugin` to detect lazy resolution (doctor.go:431-444).\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Verify `ddx init --global` creates ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx pointing into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/ and writes the global config.\n2. Verify plain `ddx init` creates the project workspace and writes nothing under plugins/.\n3. Verify `ddx doctor` renders both the \"Global Install\" and \"Project Install\" lines, including the `lazy-resolves-to-global` project status when no project install exists but the global one does.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Runtime lookup precedence implementation (sibling child — ResolvePlugin).\n- The `--global` install/update surface and project install modes (sibling children).\n- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).\n- Migrating any ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.\n- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.\n\nDEPS\n- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — initGlobal and doctor topology both route through `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`.","events_attachment":"ddx-67b477d3/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-67b477d3","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:cli","area:config","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-524be1ab","status":"closed","title":"init/doctor: `ddx init --global` umbrella setup, `ddx init` lazy (no plugin install), doctor reports both layers","updated_at":"2026-06-03T16:48:57.361307585Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:220:{"acceptance":"`ddx install --force --local \u003cpath\u003e` against a repo containing broken symlinks ONLY under gitignored paths completes successfully. The validation logic either consults .gitignore or skips well-known scratch/dotfile paths.","closing_commit_sha":"08558b9bffef71ec63fdf2485ee4f4d054d8c166","created_at":"2026-05-18T02:12:47.205231666Z","description":"## Summary\n\nObserved against ddx v0.6.2-alpha103-12-ge6379ebc5 on 2026-05-18.\n\n`ddx install \u003cplugin\u003e --force --local \u003crepo\u003e` validates ALL symlinks in the source tree, including those under gitignored paths. Result: any scratch/tmp file with a stale symlink (e.g. files written by a previous `ddx doctor` invocation under doctor/home/.codex/tmp/) breaks the install despite being gitignored and never intended to ship.\n\n## Reproduction\n\n1. In a repo with `doctor/home/.codex/tmp/` gitignored (matches `.codex`)\n2. Run any operation that creates broken symlinks under that dir (e.g. an old codex CLI install no longer present)\n3. Run `ddx install helix --force --local /path/to/helix`\n4. Install fails: `validating package structure: ... broken symlink target ...`\n\nRepro on HELIX:\n```\nddx install helix --force --local /home/erik/Projects/helix\n# Error: validating package structure:\n#   doctor/home/.codex/tmp/arg0/codex-arg0zt0PZe/apply_patch: broken symlink target ...\n```\n\nWorkaround: rm -rf the offending tmp paths and retry.\n\n## Expected behavior\n\n`ddx install --local` should skip files that are gitignored in the source repository (run via `git check-ignore -v \u003cpath\u003e` or read `.gitignore` directly). At minimum, it should skip paths the user could not have intended to ship: well-known scratch/tmp directory names, dotfiles outside the plugin manifest's declared paths, etc.\n\n## Related\n\n- Related to (but distinct from) ddx-efadca32 (lifecycle worktree empty) filed 2026-05-16.\n- Blocks HELIX's `ddx install helix --force` for any operator with tmp scratch state present.","events_attachment":"ddx-686564bf/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-686564bf","issue_type":"bug","labels":["kind:bug","area:install","area:validation","observed-failure"],"owner":"easel","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-eec2ad7d","status":"closed","title":"ddx install --local walks gitignored paths and fails validation on broken symlinks in scratch/tmp dirs","updated_at":"2026-05-27T03:18:37.806916401Z"}

Existing `TestPreClaimReadiness_DecodesFractionalScore` remains green.\n7. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... -run \"TestPreClaimReadiness_.*(Waiver|SuggestedChild|FractionalScore)|TestPreClaimReadinessPromptSpecifiesOutputShapes\" -count=1` passes.\n8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"551e487bc824ffb6b525a3fec05ec491acc8821d","created_at":"2026-05-15T05:32:32.444180509Z","description":"PROBLEM\n`ddx work --watch --harness codex --model gpt-5.4 --project /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau` still reports readiness decode failures and then fail-opens into execution. Two observed shapes are now known:\n- `waivers_applied` may be emitted as a flat string list, causing `json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field preClaimReadinessClassificationPromptResult.waivers_applied of type agent.preClaimReadinessWaiver`.\n- `suggested_child_beads[].acceptance` may be emitted as a single string, causing `json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field preClaimReadinessSuggestedChild.suggested_child_beads.acceptance of type []string`.\n\nBoth failures make the readiness model unavailable even though the model produced a semantically usable classification. This keeps readiness from contributing route/difficulty signals and produces noisy fail-open logs during normal `ddx work --watch` operation.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\n`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:60-72` decodes the readiness classification payload directly into strict Go structs. `preClaimReadinessClassificationPromptResult.SuggestedChildren` is `[]preClaimReadinessSuggestedChild`, while `preClaimReadinessSuggestedChild.Acceptance` at `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:148-152` requires `[]string`. `WaiversApplied` at `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:71` requires structured objects. `decodeReadinessClassificationPayloadWithMode` at `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:584-588` fails the whole readiness classification on any JSON shape mismatch. The prompt text at `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:424-431` asks for `suggested_child_beads` and `waivers_applied` but does not give enough defensive handling for common model scalar/list variants.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nAdd small JSON normalization types for readiness model-only fields so decoding accepts the observed variants without creating new execution paths. Specifically: normalize `waivers_applied` from omitted/null/empty array/flat string list/object list, and normalize `suggested_child_beads[].acceptance` from either a string or a string array. Keep the internal readiness result behavior unchanged unless existing code already consumes these fields. Tighten the readiness prompt so it documents the preferred shapes and explicitly says scalar fallbacks are tolerated only by the decoder.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not persist readiness waiver or child-bead suggestion data to bead metadata. Do not add numeric difficulty/score metadata. Do not implement automatic decomposition behavior here. Do not change the easy/medium/hard routing contract.","events_attachment":"ddx-689471d1/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-689471d1","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:readiness","area:routing","kind:bug","phase:2","downstream:fizeau"],"priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-1612174f","status":"closed","title":"readiness: accept model-output waiver and child-acceptance variants","updated_at":"2026-05-15T10:56:39.547961624Z"}

FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation requires the loop to diagnose every non-ready bead, fire matching remediators, and re-scan without sleeping when at least one remediation succeeded.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nNo diagnose+dispatch logic exists at the insertion point.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nInside execute_bead_loop.go, after ReadyExecutionBreakdown populates breakdown (~line 1265) and before applyStop/sleepOrWake (~line 1271), insert the idle-path auto-remediation block.\n\nPseudo-flow (translate to Go):\n1. Switch on the BlockReasonCode:\n   - superseded_pending_close: queue a no-op (the cascade happens automatically inside any subsequent Close(); to actively trigger it on a known X, call store.Close on the SUPERSEDER Y if it's somehow still open in this branch — typically not; if X is the bead and Y is already closed but the cascade never ran, call a new store.RunSupersededCascade(X, Y) helper that runs the same scope-checked cascade out of band).\n   - closure_candidate_misclassified: this should disappear once the RC-1 classifier fix lands; if it still appears, log and surface (defensive).\n   - dead_intermediate_all_children_closed: trigger the existing closure-evaluation path against the bead.\n   - dead_intermediate_open_children_pending OR epic_of_epic: skip (recursion via children handles it).\n   - genuinely_needs_decomposition: queue an AutoDecomposeEpic call. At most ONE auto-decompose dispatch per loop iteration (track a counter). Skip if --no-auto-epic-decompose set.\n   - dep_blocked_by, parent_child_state_conflict, claimed_in_progress, provider_route_unavailable, gated_by_budget_or_cooldown, malformed_parent_or_dep_ref, dependency_cycle, closed_or_missing_parent, auto_remediation_exhausted, no_diagnosis: no idle-path action; will surface via ddx work focus.\n   - stale_graph_index: trigger an index rebuild and re-diagnose next loop.\n4. Execute queued remediations sequentially. Track count of successes.\n5. If none succeeded, fall through to the existing emit(\"loop.idle\", ...) + sleepOrWake path.\n\nAdd operator override flags to cli/cmd/work.go: --no-auto-supersede-close, --no-auto-epic-decompose, --no-auto-closure-reclassify. TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject (cli/cmd/update_test.go) asserts `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree and does not modify the project install or project state.\n3. A test asserts `ddx plugin list --global` and `ddx plugin show \u003cname\u003e --global` enumerate/report from the global tier.\n4. library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md documents the global install location and agent-tier link paths.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... green.\n6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"4ac5ec29273c18d7542398a829434fd1683b8ee6","created_at":"2026-06-03T12:37:10.38876229Z","description":"PROBLEM (epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2)\n\nOperators who run one Claude Code/Codex install per machine need a `--global` surface so skills/personas install once into the machine-wide DDx tree and are discoverable from every project without per-project setup. Today the global install and update paths must write to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/ and link into the home-tier agent skill dirs, using a separate global state file so they never touch a project's install.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- `ddx install` carries the `--global` flag at cli/cmd/install.go:49 and routes through `resolveInstallDirs(global)` (install.go:184) and `selectInstallState(global)` (install.go:202). For global=true, resolveInstallDirs returns installRoot=`ddxroot.GlobalDir()`, agentSkillsDir=`~/.agents/skills`, claudeSkillsDir=`~/.claude/skills` (install.go:185-193); selectInstallState returns `registry.LoadGlobalState`/`SaveGlobalState` (install.go:206-208). Targets are remapped by `adjustInstallTargets`/`remapSkillTargets` (install.go:215,235). Global installs skip the project auto-commit (install.go:174).\n- `ddx update --global` routes through `performGlobalUpdate` (cli/cmd/update.go:300), which operates exclusively on `registry.LoadGlobalState()`/`ddxroot.GlobalDir()` (update.go:301,337,345) and never touches the project tree; the flag is parsed at update.go:395 and dispatched at update.go:66.\n- The `--global` flag must also be present on `ddx plugin list`/`ddx plugin show` so query surfaces can target the global tier.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Verify `ddx install \u003cname\u003e --global --silent` writes the plugin under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/ and creates a symlink-or-copy under ~/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e and ~/.agents/skills/\u003cname\u003e, recording the result in the global state file (not the project state).\n2. Verify `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree and leaves any project install untouched.\n3. Add/confirm `--global` on `ddx plugin list` and `ddx plugin show`.\n4. Document the global install location in library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md \"How this skill works\".\n5. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook (ddx-777f4a09) must continue to scope skill validation to staged skill files regardless of install location.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Runtime lookup precedence (sibling child — ResolvePlugin).\n- Project (non-global) install behavior and init/doctor wiring (sibling children).\n- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).\n- Migrating any ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.\n- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.\n\nDEPS\n- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — global install routes through `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`.","events_attachment":"ddx-6931d485/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-6931d485","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:cli","area:plugin","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas","triage:no-changes-unjustified"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-93a1e107","status":"closed","title":"install/update: --global surface installs to XDG global tree + agent-tier skill links","updated_at":"2026-06-03T17:16:46.067151221Z","work-no-changes-count":1}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:228:{"created_at":"2026-05-21T03:25:29.25797088Z","description":"PROBLEM\nThe docker-clone attempt backend is unusable on a remote, rootless, or de-nested Docker daemon (e.g. OrbStack on this machine, docker info Name=orbstack). Every attempt fast-fails in 17-39ms before any agent work with: docker: Error response from daemon: invalid mount config for type \"bind\": bind source path does not exist: \u003chost path\u003e. Outcome is classified execution_failed / transport.\n\nROOT CAUSE\ncli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:612 dockerRunArgs() constructs the container with five classes of HOST bind-mount (--mount type=bind,src=\u003chostpath\u003e):\n  - :622 src=ws.WorkDir            dst=/work               (cloned repo)\n  - :623 src=exe                   dst=/usr/local/bin/ddx  (ddx binary, os.Executable())\n  - :636 src=ws.DockerRun          dst=/ddx-runtime        (HOME/cache/tmp + injected creds)\n  - :641-642 gocache, work-tmp                              (Go build/test cache)\n  - :644-649 dockerToolMounts() -\u003e claude/codex/gemini      (agent CLIs via exec.LookPath)\nA de-nested/remote daemon's filesystem namespace does not include this machine's /home/erik/... , so every src path 'does not exist' from the daemon's view. Confirmed: 4 attempts on ddx-b5816493 failed this way; failing paths included /home/erik/.local/bin/ddx and /home/erik/.local/share/claude/versions/2.1.145. A short -v probe 'succeeded' only because docker silently auto-created an empty dir (the classic -v footgun), proving the source was not visible.\n\nPROPOSED FIX (decomposed into child tasks)\nAdopt the standard create -\u003e cp -\u003e start -\u003e cp-out -\u003e rm pattern used for remote/rootless daemons, plus bake static tooling into the image and use daemon-managed named volumes for caches. Only the daemon's own storage (image layers + named volumes) is reliably reachable; everything dynamic is copied in/out.\n  1. Bake agent CLIs into the attempt-runner image; drop their bind-mounts.\n  2. Keystone: move workspace + ddx binary in via docker create/cp instead of bind.\n  3. Convert Go caches + runtime dir to named volumes.\n  4. Inject credentials via env/docker cp instead of the runtime-home bind.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- The worktree and local-clone backends (unaffected; worktree is the working default).\n- Routing/provider selection.\n\nNOTE: filed from an operator session after switching the live queue drain to --attempt-backend worktree to work around this. Child tasks carry their own file:line scope and AC.","id":"ddx-6967f6e6","issue_type":"epic","labels":["area:agent","kind:bug","reliability","spec:FEAT-010"],"priority":2,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"open","title":"docker-clone attempt backend: eliminate host bind-mounts for remote/de-nested daemon compatibility","updated_at":"2026-05-21T03:25:29.25797088Z"}

This child owns part 5 of 5 of that parent scope.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nPROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)\n\nSkills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:\n\n- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e, ~/.codex/skills/\u003cname\u003e, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.\n- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/. In in-tree mode that is \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/\u003chost\u003e/\u003cowner\u003e/\u003crepo\u003e/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. \u003cproject\u003e/.claude/skills/ link target).\n\nToday `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.\n- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation.\n- The agent-tier paths (~/.claude/skills/...) are written only when the operator manually links them, or via the legacy install paths that were retired (per the user's `feedback_no_local_replace` and the home-directory-install retirement noted in CLAUDE.md).\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n\n1. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:\n   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/.\n   - Write the plugin content there.\n   - Create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e, ~/.codex/skills/\u003cname\u003e, and any others the agent registry knows about.\n   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.\n\n2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/ and links into \u003cproject\u003e/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e AND \u003cproject\u003e/.agents/skills/\u003cname\u003e. In convention mode (no \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/) the project's agent-tier paths still point into the XDG project dir's plugins. In in-tree mode they point into \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins/.\n\n3. `ddx init --global` is\n...[truncated]\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nImplement only this child acceptance slice:\n- cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... green.\n- lefthook run pre-commit passes.\n- Spec updates (ADR/skill-topology + CLAUDE.md Persona System) landed in this bead's commit.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.","events_attachment":"ddx-6a23cbd3/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-6a23cbd3","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config","area:plugin","area:cli","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas","decomposed"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-46eb6782","status":"closed","title":"skills/personas/plugins: install-to-agent vs install-in-project — and: part 5","updated_at":"2026-06-03T23:00:54.031349686Z"}

The concrete types (`*Store`, `RawBackend`, `JSONLBackend`, `ExternalBackend`, `AxonStore`, `*WatcherHub`) live at `cli/internal/bead/` — publicly importable from any other package.\n\nThe goal of TD-027 §21 is **physical impossibility** of bypass: not lint-prevented, not review-prevented, but a Go compiler error if any code outside `cli/internal/bead/` tries to import the concrete storage implementations. This bead enforces that via Go's `internal/` rule.\n\n# ROOT CAUSE\n- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` public.\n- `cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go` — `JSONLBackend` public.\n- `cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go` — `ExternalBackend` public.\n- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go` (or `AxonStore` post `ddx-9c5bca8f`) — public.\n- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` — `*WatcherHub` public.\n- All supporting concrete implementation files (`claim_liveness.go`, `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc.) — public.\n\nPlus the final assembly step (formerly \"K\" in earlier breakdowns): `Backend` composite interface declared, `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite, compile-time assertion `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`. These were deferred from Foundation because the composite couldn't be asserted until all per-concern beads removed their methods.\n\n# PROPOSED FIX\n\nThis bead does three load-bearing things in one atomic change:\n\n## 1. Narrow caller field types from `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (or narrower)\n\nEvery caller file in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that holds `*bead.Store` as a struct field or local variable is updated:\n\n- Most callers: change `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (full interface). Mechanical rename.\n- Narrow candidates (identified during per-concern beads): use narrower sub-interface where appropriate (e.g., `cli/cmd/work_focus.go` → `bead.BeadQueries`; `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go` → `bead.BeadReader`).\n- Documented exceptions remain on concrete `*Store` access OR on direct file I/O (allowlisted):\n  - `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go` — file-level repair; reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.\n  - `cli/cmd/bead.go` (git auto-commit + conflict-resolution code paths only) — operates on the file as a file.\n  - `cli/cmd/sync.go` — `ddx sync` operates on the file.\n\n## 2. Add `Backend` composite + compile-time assertion\n\n- Declare `Backend` as the union of all 11 sub-interfaces:\n  `BeadInitializer + BeadReader + BeadLifecycle + BeadEventReader + BeadEventWriter + BeadQueries + BeadDependencyReader + BeadDependencyWriter + BeadArchive + BeadInterchangeReader + BeadInterchangeWriter`.\n- Declare `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite (5 reader sub-interfaces) per TD-027 §6.1.\n- Add compile-time assertion: `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`.\n\n## 3. Move concretes to internal/\n\n- Create `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/`.\n- Move (with package rename):\n  - `store.go` → `internal/storage/store.go` (package `storage`)\n  - `backend_jsonl.go` → `internal/storage/backend_jsonl.go`\n  - `backend_external.go` → `internal/storage/backend_external.go`\n  - `axon_backend.go` / `axon_store.go` → `internal/storage/`\n  - `claim_liveness.go` → `internal/storage/`\n  - `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc. → `internal/storage/`\n  - `watcher.go` → `internal/lifecycle/watcher_hub.go` (package `lifecycle`)\n  - `RawBackend` and `OperationApplier` interfaces also move into `internal/storage/`\n- Create `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` as the SOLE public construction path:\n  ```go\n  package bead\n  import \"cli/internal/bead/internal/storage\"\n  func NewStore(opts StoreOptions) (Backend, error) { return storage.New(opts) }\n  ```\n- The public `cli/internal/bead/` package surface is now: `Bead` struct, `Backend` + sub-interfaces, `Operation` + named ops, errors, `IDGenerator` + `ValidateID`, ctx accessors, `LifecycleSubscriber` interface, `Migrator` factory (from MIG bead), and the `ops/` helper sub-packages.\n\n## 4. Lint cleanup\n\nAfter lockdown, two analyzers in the lint suite (`ddx-e91a45c0`) are redundant because Go's `internal/` rule enforces the same property at the compiler level:\n- `concrete-store-methods` — removed.\n- `no-internal-store-construction` — removed.\n\nRetained analyzers (defense-in-depth inside `cli/internal/bead/`):\n- `direct-bead-jsonl-io` (callers still shouldn't bypass the abstraction)\n- `no-new-rawbackend-impls` (new backends use Backend directly)\n- `typed-context-accessors-only` (ctx.Value discipline)\n\n# NON-SCOPE\n- Per-concern bead work — must complete first.\n- AxonStore implementation — `ddx-9c5bca8f`.\n- IMP (JSONL→Postgres importer) — separate downstream bead.\n- WIRE (real-wire integration tests) — separate downstream bead.\n\n# Acceptance criteria\n\n1. Every caller file in scope (per PROPOSED FIX §1) has its `*bead.Store` field/variable types narrowed to `bead.Backend` or a sub-interface, with the allowlist exceptions (`bead_doctor.go`, file-level paths in `bead.go`, `sync.go`).\n2. `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` declares `Backend` as the union of 11 sub-interfaces. `ReadOnlyBackend` declared per TD-027 §6.1.\n3. `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/` exist; all concrete implementation files relocated with appropriate package renames.\n4. `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` is the sole public construction path. Exports `NewStore`, `NewLifecycleSubscriber`, `NewMigrator` (from MIG). No other public function constructs a concrete storage type.\n5. **Build must succeed**: `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go build ./...`. **Failure indicates incomplete per-concern bead migration** — diagnose the failing caller and complete its migration before retrying. Do NOT bypass via build tags or temporary public re-exports.\n6. Boundary verification test: `TestModuleBoundary_NoInternalImportsOutsideBead` — static analysis scans every Go file under `cli/` outside `cli/internal/bead/` and asserts none of them import `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage` or `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle`. AST-based.\n7. Compile-time `var _ Backend = (*storage.Store)(nil)` exists inside `internal/storage/` (verifying Backend satisfaction now that the concrete type is private to that package).\n8. Lint cleanup: `concrete-store-methods` and `no-internal-store-construction` analyzers removed from the bead-lints binary. Documented in `cli/tools/lints/README.md`. The retained 3 analyzers stay.\n9. Existing tests pass without modification. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./...` is green.\n10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.\n\n# Labels\nphase:iterate, area:bead, area:storage, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027, reliability\n\n# Parent + Deps\nParent: ddx-8d747049\nDeps: all per-concern beads must complete before this bead can execute:\n- ddx-bca628fa (L)\n- ddx-e1c743d3 (C)\n- ddx-f39b41b3 (E)\n- ddx-aed7c7ab (X)\n- ddx-bc07270f (Q)\n- ddx-3af1c1a6 (D)\n- ddx-71d3a2de (A)\n- ddx-0ab16765 (MIG)\n\nPlus required infrastructure:\n- ddx-9c5bca8f (AxonStore — its concrete type is also relocated)\n- ddx-29f02cf4 (config factory — factory.go takes over construction)\n- ddx-900a8d38 (WatcherHub factory — must be migrated to BeadReader factory before lockdown)\n\n# What this earns\n\n**Physically impossible bypass enforced by the Go compiler.** Any future caller in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, etc. that tries to import `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage` fails to build. The contract becomes: callers see only the public `Backend` interface (and sub-interfaces, Operations, sentinels, IDGenerator, factory); they cannot reach the concrete type.","id":"ddx-74452926","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:bead","area:storage","kind:refactor","phase:iterate","reliability","spec:TD-027"],"notes":"DESIGN HOLD 2026-05-11: parked alongside ddx-c6317784 until TD-027/TD-031 consolidation lands and operator clears via 'ddx bead cooldown clear \u003cid\u003e'.","parent":"ddx-8d747049","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"status":"open","title":"BL (Boundary Lockdown): caller narrowing + relocate concretes to internal/storage + Backend composite + compile-time enforcement (TD-027 §21)","updated_at":"2026-05-11T20:29:34.455757285Z"}

In both modes the project-scoped agent skill dirs (`\u003cproject\u003e/.claude/skills/` and `\u003cproject\u003e/.agents/skills/`) must link into whichever plugins/ path the mode resolves to.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- For global=false, `resolveInstallDirs` (cli/cmd/install.go:184) returns installRoot=`ddxroot.Path(context.Background(), f.WorkingDir)` and skill dirs under `f.WorkingDir/.agents/skills` and `f.WorkingDir/.claude/skills` (install.go:195-197). `ddxroot.Path()` is what selects the in-tree vs convention plugins root, so the install root automatically follows the mode.\n- `ddx update` (no flag) must update the project install in whichever mode is active; the non-global path in update.go (dispatched when `opts.Global` is false, update.go:66) operates on the project working dir's resolved root.\n- Project installs auto-commit trackable project changes (install.go:174-176) — global installs do not.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Verify `ddx install \u003cname\u003e` (no --global) in in-tree mode installs to `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/` and links under `\u003cproject\u003e/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e` and `\u003cproject\u003e/.agents/skills/\u003cname\u003e`.\n2. Verify `ddx install \u003cname\u003e` (no --global) in convention mode installs to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/\u003chost\u003e/\u003cowner\u003e/\u003crepo\u003e/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/` and the project-tier links point into that XDG plugins/ path.\n3. Verify `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active and does not touch the global tree.\n4. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook (ddx-777f4a09) must continue to scope skill validation to staged skill files regardless of mode.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Runtime lookup precedence (sibling child — ResolvePlugin).\n- The `--global` install/update surface (sibling child).\n- init/doctor wiring (sibling child).\n- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).\n- Migrating any ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.\n- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.\n\nDEPS\n- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — project install root is computed by `ddxroot.Path()`, which encodes the in-tree vs convention decision.","events_attachment":"ddx-747f1b35/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-747f1b35","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:cli","area:plugin","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-b48acb35","status":"closed","title":"install/update: project install honors in-tree vs convention mode + project-tier skill links","updated_at":"2026-06-03T16:13:54.715324106Z"}
